units.text: Enter DEMETRIUS and PHILO

units.speaker: PHILO
units.text:
Nay, but this dotage of our general’s
O’erflows the measure. Those his goodly eyes,
That o’er the files and musters of the war
Have glowed like plated Mars, now bend, now turn
The office and devotion of their view
Upon a tawny front. His captain’s heart,
Which in the scuffles of great fights hath burst
The buckles on his breast, reneges all temper
And is become the bellows and the fan
To cool a gypsy’s lust.
Flourish. Enter ANTONY, CLEOPATRA, her ladies, the train, with eunuchs fanning her

units.speaker: PHILO
units.text:
Look where they come.
Take but good note, and you shall see in him
The triple pillar of the world transformed
Into a strumpet’s fool. Behold and see.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: If it be love indeed, tell me how much.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: There’s beggary in the love that can be reckoned.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: I’ll set a bourn how far to be beloved.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Then must thou needs find out new heaven, new earth.
Enter a MESSENGER

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: News, my good lord, from Rome.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Grates me, the sum.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Nay, hear them, Antony.
Fulvia perchance is angry. Or who knows
If the scarce-bearded Caesar have not sent
His powerful mandate to you, “Do this, or this.
Take in that kingdom, and enfranchise that.
Perform ’t, or else we damn thee.”

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: How, my love?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Perchance? Nay, and most like.
You must not stay here longer. Your dismission
Is come from Caesar. Therefore hear it, Antony.
Where’s Fulvia’s process? Caesar’s, I would say—both?
Call in the messengers. As I am Egypt’s queen,
Thou blushest, Antony, and that blood of thine
Is Caesar’s homager. Else so thy cheek pays shame
When shrill-tongued Fulvia scolds. The messengers!

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Let Rome in Tiber melt and the wide arch
Of the ranged empire fall. Here is my space.
Kingdoms are clay. Our dungy earth alike
Feeds beast as man. The nobleness of life
Is to do thus, when such a mutual pair
And such a twain can do ’t, in which I bind,
On pain of punishment, the world to weet
We stand up peerless.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Excellent falsehood!
Why did he marry Fulvia, and not love her?
I’ll seem the fool I am not. Antony
Will be himself.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
But stirred by Cleopatra.
Now, for the love of Love and her soft hours,
Let’s not confound the time with conference harsh.
There’s not a minute of our lives should stretch
Without some pleasure now. What sport tonight?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Hear the ambassadors.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Fie, wrangling Queen!
Whom every thing becomes—to chide, to laugh,
To weep, whose every passion fully strives
To make itself, in thee, fair and admired!
No messenger but thine, and all alone
Tonight we’ll wander through the streets and note
The qualities of people. Come, my Queen,
Last night you did desire it.— [To the MESSENGER] Speak
not to us.
Exeunt ANTONY and CLEOPATRA with the train

units.speaker: DEMETRIUS
units.text: Is Caesar with Antonius prized so slight?

units.speaker: PHILO
units.text:
Sir, sometimes when he is not Antony
He comes too short of that great property
Which still should go with Antony.

units.speaker: DEMETRIUS
units.text:
I am full sorry
That he approves the common liar, who
Thus speaks of him at Rome, but I will hope
Of better deeds tomorrow. Rest you happy!
Exeunt
Enter ENOBARBUS, LAMPRIUS, a SOOTHSAYER, Rannius, LUCILLIUS, CHARMIAN, IRAS, MARDIAN the eunuch, and ALEXAS

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
Lord Alexas, sweet Alexas, most anything Alexas, almost
most absolute Alexas, where’s the soothsayer that you
praised so to th’ Queen? Oh that I knew this husband,
which, you say, must charge his horns with garlands!

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text: Soothsayer!

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text: Your will?

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
[To ALEXAS] Is this the man? [To SOOTHSAYER] Is ’t you,
sir, that know things?

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text:
In nature’s infinite book of secrecy
A little I can read.

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text: [To CHARMIAN] Show him your hand.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
[To servants within] Bring in the banquet quickly. Wine
enough
Cleopatra’s health to drink.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
[giving hand to SOOTHSAYER] Good sir, give me good
fortune.

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text: I make not, but foresee.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Pray, then, foresee me one.

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text: You shall be yet far fairer than you are.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: [To the others] He means in flesh.

units.speaker: IRAS
units.text: No, you shall paint when you are old.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Wrinkles forbid!

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text: Vex not his prescience. Be attentive.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Hush!

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text: You shall be more beloving than beloved.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: I had rather heat my liver with drinking.

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text: Nay, hear him.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
Good now, some excellent fortune! Let me be married to
three kings in a forenoon and widow them all. Let me
have a child at fifty, to whom Herod of Jewry may do
homage. Find me to marry me with Octavius Caesar, and
companion me with my mistress.

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text: You shall outlive the lady whom you serve.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Oh, excellent! I love long life better than figs.

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text:
You have seen and proved a fairer former fortune
Than that which is to approach.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
Then belike my children shall have no names. Prithee,
how many boys and wenches must I have?

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text:
If every of your wishes had a womb,
And fertile every wish, a million.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Out, fool! I forgive thee for a witch.

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text:
You think none but your sheets are privy to your
wishes.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: [To SOOTHSAYER] Nay, come, tell Iras hers.

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text: We’ll know all our fortunes.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Mine, and most of our fortunes tonight, shall be—drunk
to bed.

units.speaker: IRAS
units.text:
[giving her hand to the SOOTHSAYER] There’s a palm
presages chastity, if nothing else.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: E’en as the o’erflowing Nilus presageth famine.

units.speaker: IRAS
units.text: Go, you wild bedfellow, you cannot soothsay.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
Nay, if an oily palm be not a fruitful prognostication,
I cannot scratch mine ear.—Prithee, tell her but a
workaday fortune.

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text: Your fortunes are alike.

units.speaker: IRAS
units.text: But how, but how? Give me particulars.

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text: I have said.

units.speaker: IRAS
units.text: Am I not an inch of fortune better than she?

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
Well, if you were but an inch of fortune better than I,
where would you choose it?

units.speaker: IRAS
units.text: Not in my husband’s nose.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
Our worser thoughts heavens mend. Alexas! [To
SOOTHSAYER] Come, his fortune, his fortune! Oh, let him
marry a woman that cannot go, sweet Isis, I beseech
thee, and let her die too, and give him a worse, and let
worse follow worse, till the worst of all follow him
laughing to his grave, fifty-fold a cuckold! Good Isis,
hear me this prayer, though thou deny me a matter of
more weight, good Isis, I beseech thee!

units.speaker: IRAS
units.text:
Amen, dear goddess, hear that prayer of the people!
For, as it is a heartbreaking to see a handsome man
loose-wived, so it is a deadly sorrow to behold a foul
knave uncuckolded. Therefore, dear Isis, keep decorum,
and fortune him accordingly.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Amen.

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text:
[To himself] Lo now, if it lay in their hands to make
me a cuckold, they would make themselves whores but
they’d do ’t.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Hush! Here comes Antony.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Not he. The Queen.
Enter CLEOPATRA

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Saw you my lord?

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: No, lady.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Was he not here?

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: No, madam.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
He was disposed to mirth, but on the sudden
A Roman thought hath struck him.—Enobarbus!

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Madam?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Seek him and bring him hither.—
Where’s Alexas?

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text: Here at your service. My lord approaches.
Enter ANTONY with the FIRST MESSENGER

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: We will not look upon him. Go with us.
Exeunt all but ANTONY and the FIRST MESSENGER

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text: Fulvia thy wife first came into the field.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Against my brother Lucius?

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text:
Ay.
But soon that war had end, and the time’s state
Made friends of them, joining their force ’gainst
Caesar,
Whose better issue in the war from Italy
Upon the first encounter drave them.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Well, what worst?

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text: The nature of bad news infects the teller.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
When it concerns the fool or coward. On.
Things that are past are done, with me. ’Tis thus:
Who tells me true, though in his tale lie death,
I hear him as he flattered.

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text:
Labienus—
This is stiff news—hath with his Parthian force
Extended Asia: from Euphrates
His conquering banner shook, from Syria
To Lydia and to Ionia,
Whilst—

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: “Antony,” thou wouldst say.

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text: O my lord!

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Speak to me home. Mince not the general tongue.
Name Cleopatra as she is called in Rome.
Rail thou in Fulvia’s phrase, and taunt my faults
With such full license as both truth and malice
Have power to utter. Oh, then we bring forth weeds
When our quick minds lie still, and our ills told us
Is as our earing.
Enter SECOND MESSENGER

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Fare thee well awhile.

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text: At your noble pleasure.
Exit FIRST MESSENGER

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: From Sicyon, how, the news? Speak there.

units.speaker: SECOND MESSENGER
units.text: The man from Sicyon—

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Is there such an one?

units.speaker: SECOND MESSENGER
units.text: He stays upon your will.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Let him appear.
Exit SECONDMESSENGER

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
These strong Egyptian fetters I must break,
Or lose myself in dotage.
Enter THIRD MESSENGER, with a letter

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: What are you?

units.speaker: THIRD MESSENGER
units.text: Fulvia thy wife is dead.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Where died she?

units.speaker: THIRD MESSENGER
units.text:
In Sicyon.
Her length of sickness, with what else more serious
Importeth thee to know, this bears.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Forbear me.
Exit THIRD MESSENGER

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
[To himself] There’s a great spirit gone! Thus did I
desire it.
What our contempts doth often hurl from us
We wish it ours again. The present pleasure,
By revolution lowering, does become
The opposite of itself. She’s good, being gone.
The hand could pluck her back that shoved her on.
I must from this enchanting Queen break off.
Ten thousand harms, more than the ills I know
My idleness doth hatch. —How now, Enobarbus!
Enter ENOBARBUS

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: What’s your pleasure, sir?

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: I must with haste from hence.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Why, then, we kill all our women. We see how mortal an
unkindness is to them. If they suffer our departure,
death’s the word.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: I must be gone.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Under a compelling occasion, let women die. It were
pity to cast them away for nothing, though between them
and a great cause they should be esteemed nothing.
Cleopatra, catching but the least noise of this, dies
instantly. I have seen her die twenty times upon far
poorer moment. I do think there is mettle in death,
which commits some loving act upon her, she hath such a
celerity in dying.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: She is cunning past man’s thought.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Alack, sir, no, her passions are made of nothing but
the finest part of pure love. We cannot call her winds
and waters sighs and tears. They are greater storms and
tempests than almanacs can report. This cannot be
cunning in her. If it be, she makes a shower of rain as
well as Jove.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Would I had never seen her!

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
O sir, you had then left unseen a wonderful piece of
work which not to have been blessed withal would have
discredited your travel.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Fulvia is dead.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Sir?

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Fulvia is dead.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Fulvia?

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Dead.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Why, sir, give the gods a thankful sacrifice. When it
pleaseth their deities to take the wife of a man from
him, it shows to man the tailors of the earth,
comforting therein, that when old robes are worn out,
there are members to make new. If there were no more
women but Fulvia, then had you indeed a cut, and the
case to be lamented. This grief is crowned with
consolation . Your old smock brings forth a new
petticoat, and indeed the tears live in an onion that
should water this sorrow.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
The business she hath broached in the state
Cannot endure my absence.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
And the business you have broached here cannot be
without you, especially that of Cleopatra’s, which
wholly depends on your abode.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
No more light answers. Let our officers
Have notice what we purpose. I shall break
The cause of our expedience to the Queen
And get her leave to part. For not alone
The death of Fulvia, with more urgent touches,
Do strongly speak to us, but the letters too
Of many our contriving friends in Rome
Petition us at home. Sextus Pompeius
Hath given the dare to Caesar and commands
The empire of the sea. Our slippery people,
Whose love is never linked to the deserver
Till his deserts are past, begin to throw
Pompey the Great and all his dignities
Upon his son, who—high in name and power,
Higher than both in blood and life—stands up
For the main soldier, whose quality, going on,
The sides o’ th’ world may danger. Much is breeding
Which, like the courser’s hair, hath yet but life,
And not a serpent’s poison. Say our pleasure,
To such whose place is under us, requires
Our quick remove from hence.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: I shall do ’t.
Exeunt
Enter CLEOPATRA, CHARMIAN, ALEXAS, and IRAS

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Where is he?

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: I did not see him since.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
[To ALEXAS] See where he is, who’s with him, what he
does.
I did not send you. If you find him sad,
Say I am dancing. If in mirth, report
That I am sudden sick. Quick, and return.
Exit ALEXAS

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
Madam, methinks, if you did love him dearly,
You do not hold the method to enforce
The like from him.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: What should I do I do not?

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: In each thing give him way. Cross him in nothing.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Thou teachest like a fool the way to lose him.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
Tempt him not so too far. I wish, forbear.
In time we hate that which we often fear.
Enter ANTONY

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: But here comes Antony.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: I am sick and sullen.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: I am sorry to give breathing to my purpose—

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Help me away, dear Charmian! I shall fall.
It cannot be thus long. The sides of nature
Will not sustain it.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Now, my dearest Queen—

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Pray you, stand farther from me.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: What’s the matter?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
I know by that same eye there’s some good news.
What, says the married woman you may go?
Would she had never given you leave to come!
Let her not say ’tis I that keep you here.
I have no power upon you. Hers you are.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: The gods best know—

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Oh never was there queen
So mightily betrayed! Yet at the first
I saw the treasons planted.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Cleopatra—

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Why should I think you can be mine, and true—
Though you in swearing shake the thronèd gods—
Who have been false to Fulvia? Riotous madness,
To be entangled with those mouth-made vows
Which break themselves in swearing!

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Most sweet Queen—

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Nay, pray you, seek no color for your going,
But bid farewell and go. When you sued staying,
Then was the time for words. No going then!
Eternity was in our lips and eyes,
Bliss in our brows’ bent, none our parts so poor
But was a race of heaven. They are so still,
Or thou, the greatest soldier of the world,
Art turned the greatest liar.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: How now, lady?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
I would I had thy inches. Thou shouldst know
There were a heart in Egypt.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Hear me, Queen:
The strong necessity of time commands
Our services awhile, but my full heart
Remains in use with you. Our Italy
Shines o’er with civil swords. Sextus Pompeius
Makes his approaches to the port of Rome.
Equality of two domestic powers
Breed scrupulous faction. The hated, grown to strength,
Are newly grown to love. The condemned Pompey,
Rich in his father’s honor, creeps apace
Into the hearts of such as have not thrived
Upon the present state, whose numbers threaten;
And quietness, grown sick of rest, would purge
By any desperate change. My more particular,
And that which most with you should safe my going,
Is Fulvia’s death.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Though age from folly could not give me freedom,
It does from childishness. Can Fulvia die?

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: She’s dead, my Queen.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
O most false love!
Where be the sacred vials thou shouldst fill
With sorrowful water? Now I see, I see,
In Fulvia’s death how mine received shall be.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Quarrel no more, but be prepared to know
The purposes I bear, which are or cease
As you shall give th’ advice. By the fire
That quickens Nilus’ slime, I go from hence
Thy soldier, servant, making peace or war
As thou affects.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Cut my lace, Charmian, come!
But let it be. I am quickly ill, and well,
So Antony loves.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
My precious Queen, forbear,
And give true evidence to his love which stands
An honorable trial.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
So Fulvia told me.
I prithee, turn aside and weep for her.
Then bid adieu to me, and say the tears
Belong to Egypt. Good now, play one scene
Of excellent dissembling, and let it look
Like perfect honor.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: You’ll heat my blood. No more.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: You can do better yet, but this is meetly.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Now, by my sword—

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
And target. Still he mends.
[To CHARMIAN] But this is not the best. Look, prithee,
Charmian,
How this Herculean Roman does become
The carriage of his chafe.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: I’ll leave you, lady.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Courteous lord, one word.
Sir, you and I must part, but that’s not it.
Sir, you and I have loved, but there’s not it,
That you know well. Something it is I would—
Oh, my oblivion is a very Antony,
And I am all forgotten.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
But that your royalty
Holds idleness your subject, I should take you
For idleness itself.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
’Tis sweating labor
To bear such idleness so near the heart
As Cleopatra this. But, sir, forgive me,
Since my becomings kill me when they do not
Eye well to you. Your honor calls you hence.
Therefore be deaf to my unpitied folly,
And all the gods go with you! Upon your sword
Sit laurel victory, and smooth success
Be strewed before your feet.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Let us go. Come.
Our separation so abides and flies
That thou, residing here, goes yet with me,
And I, hence fleeting, here remain with thee.
Away!
Exeunt
Enter OCTAVIUSCAESAR, reading a letter, LEPIDUS, and their train

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
You may see, Lepidus, and henceforth know,
It is not Caesar’s natural vice to hate
Our great competitor. From Alexandria
This is the news: he fishes, drinks, and wastes
The lamps of night in revel; is not more manlike
Than Cleopatra, nor the queen of Ptolemy
More womanly than he; hardly gave audience, or
Vouchsafed to think he had partners. You shall find
there
A man who is th’ abstract of all faults
That all men follow.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
I must not think there are
Evils enough to darken all his goodness.
His faults in him seem as the spots of heaven,
More fiery by night’s blackness, hereditary
Rather than purchased, what he cannot change
Than what he chooses.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
You are too indulgent. Let’s grant, it is not
Amiss to tumble on the bed of Ptolemy,
To give a kingdom for a mirth, to sit
And keep the turn of tippling with a slave,
To reel the streets at noon, and stand the buffet
With knaves that smell of sweat. Say this becomes him—
As his composure must be rare indeed
Whom these things cannot blemish—yet must Antony
No way excuse his foils when we do bear
So great weight in his lightness. If he filled
His vacancy with his voluptuousness,
Full surfeits and the dryness of his bones
Call on him for ’t. But to confound such time
That drums him from his sport and speaks as loud
As his own state and ours, ’tis to be chid
As we rate boys who, being mature in knowledge,
Pawn their experience to their present pleasure
And so rebel to judgment.
Enter FIRST MESSENGER

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: Here’s more news.

units.speaker: FIRST MESSENGER
units.text:
Thy biddings have been done, and every hour,
Most noble Caesar, shalt thou have report
How ’tis abroad. Pompey is strong at sea,
And it appears he is beloved of those
That only have feared Caesar. To the ports
The discontents repair, and men’s reports
Give him much wronged.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
I should have known no less.
It hath been taught us from the primal state
That he which is was wished until he were,
And the ebbed man, ne’er loved till ne’er worth love,
Comes deared by being lacked. This common body,
Like to a vagabond flag upon the stream,
Goes to and back, lackeying the varying tide
To rot itself with motion.
Enter SECOND MESSENGER

units.speaker: SECOND MESSENGER
units.text:
Caesar, I bring thee word
Menecrates and Menas, famous pirates,
Make the sea serve them, which they ear and wound
With keels of every kind. Many hot inroads
They make in Italy—the borders maritime
Lack blood to think on ’t—and flush youth revolt.
No vessel can peep forth, but ’tis as soon
Taken as seen, for Pompey’s name strikes more
Than could his war resisted.
Exit

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
Antony,
Leave thy lascivious wassails. When thou once
Wast beaten from Modena, where thou slew’st
Hirtius and Pansa, consuls, at thy heel
Did famine follow, whom thou fought’st against,
Though daintily brought up, with patience more
Than savages could suffer. Thou didst drink
The stale of horses and the gilded puddle
Which beasts would cough at. Thy palate then did deign
The roughest berry on the rudest hedge.
Yea, like the stag, when snow the pasture sheets,
The barks of trees thou browsèd. On the Alps
It is reported thou didst eat strange flesh,
Which some did die to look on. And all this—
It wounds thine honor that I speak it now—
Was borne so like a soldier, that thy cheek
So much as lanked not.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: ’Tis pity of him.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
Let his shames quickly
Drive him to Rome. ’Tis time we twain
Did show ourselves i’ th’ field, and to that end
Assemble we immediate council. Pompey
Thrives in our idleness.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Tomorrow, Caesar,
I shall be furnished to inform you rightly
Both what by sea and land I can be able
To front this present time.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
Till which encounter
It is my business too. Farewell.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Farewell, my lord. What you shall know meantime
Of stirs abroad, I shall beseech you, sir,
To let me be partaker.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: Doubt not, sir. I knew it for my bond.
Exeunt
Enter CLEOPATRA, CHARMIAN, IRAS, and MARDIAN

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Charmian!

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Madam?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Ha, ha! Give me to drink mandragora.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Why, madam?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
That I might sleep out this great gap of time
My Antony is away.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: You think of him too much.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Oh, ’tis treason!

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Madam, I trust, not so.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Thou, eunuch Mardian!

units.speaker: MARDIAN
units.text: What’s your highness’ pleasure?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Not now to hear thee sing. I take no pleasure
In aught an eunuch has. ’Tis well for thee
That, being unseminared, thy freer thoughts
May not fly forth of Egypt. Hast thou affections?

units.speaker: MARDIAN
units.text: Yes, gracious madam.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Indeed?

units.speaker: MARDIAN
units.text:
Not in deed, madam, for I can do nothing
But what indeed is honest to be done.
Yet have I fierce affections, and think
What Venus did with Mars.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
O Charmian,
Where think’st thou he is now? Stands he or sits he?
Or does he walk? Or is he on his horse?
O happy horse, to bear the weight of Antony!
Do bravely, horse, for wott’st thou whom thou mov’st?
The demi-Atlas of this earth, the arm
And burgonet of men. He’s speaking now,
Or murmuring “Where’s my serpent of old Nile?”
For so he calls me. Now I feed myself
With most delicious poison. Think on me,
That am with Phoebus’ amorous pinches black
And wrinkled deep in time. Broad-fronted Caesar,
When thou wast here above the ground, I was
A morsel for a monarch. And great Pompey
Would stand and make his eyes grow in my brow.
There would he anchor his aspect, and die
With looking on his life.
Enter ALEXAS

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text: Sovereign of Egypt, hail!

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
How much unlike art thou Mark Antony!
Yet, coming from him, that great med’cine hath
With his tinct gilded thee.
How goes it with my brave Mark Antony?

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text:
Last thing he did, dear Queen,
He kissed—the last of many doubled kisses—
This orient pearl.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Mine ear must pluck it thence.

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text:
“Good friend,” quoth he,
“Say the firm Roman to great Egypt sends
This treasure of an oyster, at whose foot,
To mend the petty present, I will piece
Her opulent throne with kingdoms. All the East,
Say thou, shall call her mistress.” So he nodded,
And soberly did mount an arm-gaunt steed,
Who neighed so high that what I would have spoke
Was beastly dumbed by him.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: What was he, sad or merry?

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text:
Like to the time o’ th’ year between the extremes
Of hot and cold, he was nor sad nor merry.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
O well-divided disposition! Note him,
Note him, good Charmian, ’tis the man, but note him.
He was not sad, for he would shine on those
That make their looks by his. He was not merry,
Which seemed to tell them his remembrance lay
In Egypt with his joy, but between both.
O heavenly mingle! Be’st thou sad or merry,
The violence of either thee becomes,
So does it no man else.—Mett’st thou my posts?

units.speaker: ALEXAS
units.text:
Ay, madam, twenty several messengers.
Why do you send so thick?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Who’s born that day
When I forget to send to Antony
Shall die a beggar. Ink and paper, Charmian.
Welcome, my good Alexas. Did I, Charmian,
Ever love Caesar so?

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Oh, that brave Caesar!

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Be choked with such another emphasis!
Say, “the brave Antony.”

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: The valiant Caesar!

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
By Isis, I will give thee bloody teeth
If thou with Caesar paragon again
My man of men.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
By your most gracious pardon,
I sing but after you.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
My salad days,
When I was green in judgment, cold in blood,
To say as I said then. [To everyone] But, come, away.
[To CHARMIAN] Get me ink and paper.
He shall have every day a several greeting,
Or I’ll unpeople Egypt.
Exeunt
Enter POMPEY, MENECRATES, and MENAS, in warlike manner

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
If the great gods be just, they shall assist
The deeds of justest men.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
Know, worthy Pompey,
That what they do delay, they not deny.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
Whiles we are suitors to their throne, decays
The thing we sue for.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
We, ignorant of ourselves,
Beg often our own harms, which the wise powers
Deny us for our good, so find we profit
By losing of our prayers.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
I shall do well.
The people love me, and the sea is mine.
My powers are crescent, and my auguring hope
Says it will come to th’ full. Mark Antony
In Egypt sits at dinner, and will make
No wars without doors. Caesar gets money where
He loses hearts. Lepidus flatters both,
Of both is flattered, but he neither loves,
Nor either cares for him.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
Caesar and Lepidus
Are in the field. A mighty strength they carry.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: Where have you this? ’Tis false.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: From Silvius, sir.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
He dreams. I know they are in Rome together
Looking for Antony. But all the charms of love,
Salt Cleopatra, soften thy wanned lip!
Let witchcraft join with beauty, lust with both.
Tie up the libertine in a field of feasts,
Keep his brain fuming. Epicurean cooks,
Sharpen with cloyless sauce his appetite,
That sleep and feeding may prorogue his honor
Even till a Lethe’d dulness—
Enter VARRIUS

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: How now, Varrius?

units.speaker: VARRIUS
units.text:
This is most certain that I shall deliver:
Mark Antony is every hour in Rome
Expected. Since he went from Egypt ’tis
A space for farther travel.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
I could have given less matter
A better ear.—Menas, I did not think
This amorous surfeiter would have donned his helm
For such a petty war. His soldiership
Is twice the other twain. But let us rear
The higher our opinion, that our stirring
Can from the lap of Egypt’s widow pluck
The ne’er lust-wearied Antony.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
I cannot hope
Caesar and Antony shall well greet together.
His wife that’s dead did trespasses to Caesar.
His brother warred upon him, although, I think,
Not moved by Antony.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
I know not, Menas,
How lesser enmities may give way to greater.
Were ’t not that we stand up against them all,
’Twere pregnant they should square between themselves,
For they have entertainèd cause enough
To draw their swords. But how the fear of us
May cement their divisions and bind up
The petty difference, we yet not know.
Be ’t as our gods will have ’t. It only stands
Our lives upon to use our strongest hands.
Come, Menas.
Exeunt
Enter ENOBARBUS and LEPIDUS

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Good Enobarbus, ’tis a worthy deed,
And shall become you well, to entreat your captain
To soft and gentle speech.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
I shall entreat him
To answer like himself. If Caesar move him,
Let Antony look over Caesar’s head
And speak as loud as Mars. By Jupiter,
Were I the wearer of Antonio’s beard,
I would not shave ’t today.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: ’Tis not a time for private stomaching.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Every time serves for the matter that is then born in
’t.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: But small to greater matters must give way.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Not if the small come first.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Your speech is passion. But pray you stir
No embers up. Here comes the noble Antony.
Enter ANTONY and VENTIDIUS

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: And yonder, Caesar.
Enter OCTAVIUS CAESAR, MECAENAS, and AGRIPPA

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
[To VENTIDIUS] If we compose well here, to Parthia.
Hark, Ventidius.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: [To MECAENAS] I do not know, Maecenas. Ask Agrippa.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
[To CAESAR and ANTONY] Noble friends,
That which combined us was most great, and let not
A leaner action rend us. What’s amiss,
May it be gently heard. When we debate
Our trivial difference loud, we do commit
Murder in healing wounds. Then, noble partners,
The rather for I earnestly beseech,
Touch you the sourest points with sweetest terms,
Nor curstness grow to th’ matter.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
’Tis spoken well.
Were we before our armies, and to fight,
I should do thus.
Flourish

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: Welcome to Rome.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Thank you.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: Sit.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Sit, sir.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: Nay, then.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
I learn, you take things ill which are not so,
Or being, concern you not.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
I must be laughed at
If or for nothing or a little, I
Should say myself offended, and with you
Chiefly i’ th’ world; more laughed at, that I should
Once name you derogately, when to sound your name
It not concerned me.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: My being in Egypt, Caesar, what was ’t to you?

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
No more than my residing here at Rome
Might be to you in Egypt. Yet if you there
Did practice on my state, your being in Egypt
Might be my question.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: How intend you, “practiced”?

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
You may be pleased to catch at mine intent
By what did here befall me. Your wife and brother
Made wars upon me, and their contestation
Was theme for you. You were the word of war.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
You do mistake your business. My brother never
Did urge me in his act. I did inquire it,
And have my learning from some true reports
That drew their swords with you. Did he not rather
Discredit my authority with yours,
And make the wars alike against my stomach,
Having alike your cause? Of this my letters
Before did satisfy you. If you’ll patch a quarrel,
As matter whole you have to make it with,
It must not be with this.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
You praise yourself
By laying defects of judgment to me, but
You patched up your excuses.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Not so, not so.
I know you could not lack, I am certain on ’t,
Very necessity of this thought, that I,
Your partner in the cause ’gainst which he fought,
Could not with graceful eyes attend those wars
Which fronted mine own peace. As for my wife,
I would you had her spirit in such another.
The third o’ th’ world is yours, which with a snaffle
You may pace easy, but not such a wife.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Would we had all such wives, that the men might go to
wars with the women!

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
So much uncurbable, her garboils, Caesar,
Made out of her impatience—which not wanted
Shrewdness of policy too— I grieving grant
Did you too much disquiet. For that you must
But say I could not help it.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
I wrote to you
When rioting in Alexandria. You
Did pocket up my letters and with taunts
Did gibe my missive out of audience.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Sir,
He fell upon me ere admitted, then.
Three kings I had newly feasted, and did want
Of what I was i’ th’ morning. But next day
I told him of myself, which was as much
As to have asked him pardon. Let this fellow
Be nothing of our strife. If we contend,
Out of our question wipe him.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
You have broken
The article of your oath, which you shall never
Have tongue to charge me with.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: Soft, Caesar.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
No, Lepidus, let him speak.
The honor is sacred which he talks on now,
Supposing that I lacked it. —But, on, Caesar.
The article of my oath?

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
To lend me arms and aid when I required them,
The which you both denied.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Neglected, rather,
And then when poisoned hours had bound me up
From mine own knowledge. As nearly as I may
I’ll play the penitent to you, but mine honesty
Shall not make poor my greatness nor my power
Work without it. Truth is that Fulvia,
To have me out of Egypt, made wars here,
For which myself, the ignorant motive, do
So far ask pardon as befits mine honor
To stoop in such a case.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: ’Tis noble spoken.

units.speaker: MAECENAS
units.text:
If it might please you to enforce no further
The griefs between ye, to forget them quite
Were to remember that the present need
Speaks to atone you.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: Worthily spoken, Maecenas.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Or, if you borrow one another’s love for the instant,
you may, when you hear no more words of Pompey, return
it again. You shall have time to wrangle in when you
have nothing else to do.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Thou art a soldier only. Speak no more.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: That truth should be silent I had almost forgot.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: You wrong this presence. Therefore speak no more.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Go to, then. Your considerate stone.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
I do not much dislike the matter, but
The manner of his speech, for ’t cannot be
We shall remain in friendship, our conditions
So diff’ring in their acts. Yet if I knew
What hoop should hold us stanch, from edge to edge
O’ th’ world I would pursue it.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: Give me leave, Caesar.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: Speak, Agrippa.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text:
Thou hast a sister by the mother’s side,
Admired Octavia. Great Mark Antony
Is now a widower.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
Say not so, Agrippa.
If Cleopatra heard you, your reproof
Were well deserved of rashness.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
I am not married, Caesar. Let me hear
Agrippa further speak.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text:
To hold you in perpetual amity,
To make you brothers, and to knit your hearts
With an unslipping knot, take Antony
Octavia to his wife, whose beauty claims
No worse a husband than the best of men,
Whose virtue and whose general graces speak
That which none else can utter. By this marriage,
All little jealousies, which now seem great,
And all great fears, which now import their dangers,
Would then be nothing. Truths would be tales,
Where now half-tales be truths. Her love to both
Would each to other and all loves to both
Draw after her. Pardon what I have spoke,
For ’tis a studied, not a present thought,
By duty ruminated.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Will Caesar speak?

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
Not till he hears how Antony is touched
With what is spoke already.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
What power is in Agrippa
If I would say, “Agrippa, be it so,”
To make this good?

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
The power of Caesar, and
His power unto Octavia.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
May I never
To this good purpose, that so fairly shows,
Dream of impediment! Let me have thy hand
Further this act of grace, and from this hour
The heart of brothers govern in our loves
And sway our great designs!

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: There’s my hand.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: Happily, amen!

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
I did not think to draw my sword ’gainst Pompey,
For he hath laid strange courtesies and great
Of late upon me. I must thank him only,
Lest my remembrance suffer ill report;
At heel of that, defy him.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Time calls upon ’s.
Of us must Pompey presently be sought,
Or else he seeks out us.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Where lies he?

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: About the Mount Misena.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: What is his strength by land?

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
Great and increasing.
But by sea he is an absolute master.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
So is the fame.
Would we had spoke together! Haste we for it.
Yet, ere we put ourselves in arms, dispatch we
The business we have talked of.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
With most gladness,
And do invite you to my sister’s view,
Whither straight I’ll lead you.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Let us, Lepidus, not lack your company.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: Noble Antony, not sickness should detain me.
Flourish. Exeunt all but ENOBARBUS, AGRIPPA, and MAECENAS

units.speaker: MAECENAS
units.text: [To ENOBARBUS] Welcome from Egypt, sir.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Half the heart of Caesar, worthy Maecenas! My honorable
friend, Agrippa.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: Good Enobarbus!

units.speaker: MAECENAS
units.text:
We have cause to be glad that matters are so well
digested.
You stayed well by ’t in Egypt.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Ay, sir, we did sleep day out of countenance and made
the night light with drinking.

units.speaker: MAECENAS
units.text:
Eight wild boars roasted whole at a breakfast—and but
twelve persons there! Is this true?

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
This was but as a fly by an eagle. We had much more
monstrous matter of feast, which worthily deserved
noting.

units.speaker: MAECENAS
units.text:
She’s a most triumphant lady, if report be square to
her.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
When she first met Mark Antony, she pursed up his heart
upon the river of Cydnus.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text:
There she appeared indeed, or my reporter devised well
for her.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
I will tell you.
The barge she sat in, like a burnished throne,
Burned on the water. The poop was beaten gold,
Purple the sails, and so perfumèd that
The winds were lovesick with them. The oars were
silver,
Which to the tune of flutes kept stroke, and made
The water which they beat to follow faster,
As amorous of their strokes. For her own person,
It beggared all description: she did lie
In her pavilion—cloth-of-gold, of tissue—
O’erpicturing that Venus where we see
The fancy outwork nature. On each side her
Stood pretty dimpled boys, like smiling Cupids,
With divers-colored fans, whose wind did seem
To glow the delicate cheeks which they did cool,
And what they undid did.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: Oh, rare for Antony!

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Her gentlewomen, like the Nereides,
So many mermaids, tended her i’ th’ eyes,
And made their bends adornings. At the helm
A seeming mermaid steers. The silken tackle
Swell with the touches of those flower-soft hands
That yarely frame the office. From the barge
A strange invisible perfume hits the sense
Of the adjacent wharfs. The city cast
Her people out upon her, and Antony,
Enthroned i’ th’ marketplace, did sit alone,
Whistling to th’ air, which, but for vacancy,
Had gone to gaze on Cleopatra too
And made a gap in nature.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: Rare Egyptian!

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Upon her landing, Antony sent to her,
Invited her to supper. She replied
It should be better he became her guest,
Which she entreated. Our courteous Antony,
Whom ne’er the word of “No” woman heard speak,
Being barbered ten times o’er, goes to the feast,
And for his ordinary pays his heart
For what his eyes eat only.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text:
Royal wench!
She made great Caesar lay his sword to bed.
He plowed her, and she cropped.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
I saw her once
Hop forty paces through the public street,
And having lost her breath, she spoke, and panted,
That she did make defect perfection,
And, breathless, pour breathe forth.

units.speaker: MAECENAS
units.text: Now Antony must leave her utterly.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Never. He will not.
Age cannot wither her, nor custom stale
Her infinite variety. Other women cloy
The appetites they feed, but she makes hungry
Where most she satisfies, for vilest things
Become themselves in her, that the holy priests
Bless her when she is riggish.

units.speaker: MAECENAS
units.text:
If beauty, wisdom, modesty, can settle
The heart of Antony, Octavia is
A blessèd lottery to him.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text:
Let us go.
Good Enobarbus, make yourself my guest
Whilst you abide here.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Humbly, sir, I thank you.
Exeunt
Enter ANTONY, CAESAR; OCTAVIA between them

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
[To OCTAVIA] The world and my great office will
sometimes
Divide me from your bosom.

units.speaker: OCTAVIA
units.text:
All which time
Before the gods my knee shall bow my prayers
To them for you.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
[To CAESAR] Good night, sir. —My Octavia,
Read not my blemishes in the world’s report.
I have not kept my square, but that to come
Shall all be done by th’ rule. Good night, dear lady.
[To CAESAR] Good night, sir.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: Good night.
He exits with OCTAVIA
Enter SOOTHSAYER

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Now, sirrah, you do wish yourself in Egypt?

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text: Would I had never come from thence, nor you thither.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: If you can, your reason?

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text:
I see it in my motion, have it not in my tongue. But
yet hie you to Egypt again.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Say to me whose fortunes shall rise higher,
Caesar’s or mine?

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text:
Caesar’s.
Therefore, O Antony, stay not by his side.
Thy dæmon—that thy spirit which keeps thee—is
Noble, courageous, high, unmatchable
Where Caesar’s is not. But near him thy angel
Becomes afeard, as being o’erpowered. Therefore
Make space enough between you.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Speak this no more.

units.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
units.text:
To none but thee, no more but when to thee.
If thou dost play with him at any game,
Thou art sure to lose, and of that natural luck
He beats thee ’gainst the odds. Thy luster thickens
When he shines by. I say again, thy spirit
Is all afraid to govern thee near him,
But, he away, ’tis noble.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Get thee gone.
Say to Ventidius I would speak with him.
Exit SOOTHSAYER

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
[To himself] He shall to Parthia. Be it art or hap,
He hath spoken true. The very dice obey him,
And in our sports my better cunning faints
Under his chance. If we draw lots, he speeds.
His cocks do win the battle still of mine
When it is all to naught, and his quails ever
Beat mine, inhooped, at odds. I will to Egypt.
And though I make this marriage for my peace,
I’ th’ East my pleasure lies.
Enter VENTIDIUS

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
O come, Ventidius.
You must to Parthia. Your commission’s ready.
Follow me and receive ’t.
Exeunt
Enter LEPIDUS, MECAENAS, and AGRIPPA

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Trouble yourselves no further. Pray you, hasten
Your generals after.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text:
Sir, Mark Antony
Will e’en but kiss Octavia, and we’ll follow.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Till I shall see you in your soldiers’ dress,
Which will become you both, farewell.

units.speaker: MAECENAS
units.text:
We shall,
As I conceive the journey, be at the Mount
Before you, Lepidus.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Your way is shorter.
My purposes do draw me much about.
You’ll win two days upon me.

units.speaker: MAECENAS AGRIPPA
units.text: Sir, good success.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: Farewell.
Exeunt
Enter CLEOPATRA, CHARMIAN, IRAS, and ALEXAS

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Give me some music. Music, moody food
Of us that trade in love.

units.speaker: ALL
units.text: The music, ho!
Enter MARDIAN the eunuch

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Let it alone. Let’s to billiards. Come, Charmian.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: My arm is sore. Best play with Mardian.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
As well a woman with an eunuch played
As with a woman.—Come, you’ll play with me, sir?

units.speaker: MARDIAN
units.text: As well as I can, madam.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
And when good will is showed, though ’t come too short,
The actor may plead pardon. I’ll none now.
Give me mine angle. We’ll to th’ river. There,
My music playing far off, I will betray
Tawny-finned fishes. My bended hook shall pierce
Their slimy jaws, and as I draw them up
I’ll think them every one an Antony
And say, “Aha! You’re caught.”

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
’Twas merry when
You wagered on your angling, when your diver
Did hang a salt fish on his hook, which he
With fervency drew up.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
That time—Oh, times!—
I laughed him out of patience, and that night
I laughed him into patience. And next morn,
Ere the ninth hour, I drunk him to his bed,
Then put my tires and mantles on him, whilst
I wore his sword Philippan.
Enter a MESSENGER

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Oh, from Italy!
Ram thou thy fruitful tidings in mine ears,
That long time have been barren.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Madam, madam—

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Antonio’s dead! If thou say so, villain,
Thou kill’st thy mistress. But well and free,
If thou so yield him, there is gold, and here
My bluest veins to kiss —a hand that kings
Have lipped, and trembled kissing.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: First, madam, he is well.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Why, there’s more gold. But, sirrah, mark, we use
To say the dead are well. Bring it to that,
The gold I give thee will I melt and pour
Down thy ill-uttering throat.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Good madam, hear me.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Well, go to, I will.
But there’s no goodness in thy face—if Antony
Be free and healthful, so tart a favor
To trumpet such good tidings! If not well,
Thou shouldst come like a Fury crowned with snakes,
Not like a formal man.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Will ’t please you hear me?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
I have a mind to strike thee ere thou speak’st.
Yet if thou say Antony lives, is well,
Or friends with Caesar, or not captive to him,
I’ll set thee in a shower of gold and hail
Rich pearls upon thee.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Madam, he’s well.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Well said.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: And friends with Caesar.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Th’ art an honest man.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Caesar and he are greater friends than ever.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Make thee a fortune from me.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: But yet, madam—

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
I do not like “But yet.” It does allay
The good precedence. Fie upon “But yet.”
“But yet” is as a jailer to bring forth
Some monstrous malefactor. Prithee, friend,
Pour out the pack of matter to mine ear,
The good and bad together. He’s friends with Caesar,
In state of health, thou say’st, and, thou say’st,
free.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text:
Free, madam, no. I made no such report.
He’s bound unto Octavia.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: For what good turn?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: For the best turn i’ th’ bed.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: I am pale, Charmian.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Madam, he’s married to Octavia.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: The most infectious pestilence upon thee!

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Good madam, patience.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: What say you?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text:
Gracious madam,
I that do bring the news made not the match.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Say ’tis not so, a province I will give thee
And make thy fortunes proud. The blow thou hadst
Shall make thy peace for moving me to rage,
And I will boot thee with what gift beside
Thy modesty can beg.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: He’s married, madam.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: Rogue, thou hast lived too long.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text:
Nay then, I’ll run.
What mean you, madam? I have made no fault.
Exit

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text:
Good madam, keep yourself within yourself.
The man is innocent.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Some innocents ’scape not the thunderbolt.
Melt Egypt into Nile, and kindly creatures
Turn all to serpents. Call the slave again.
Though I am mad, I will not bite him. Call!

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: He is afeard to come.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
I will not hurt him.
These hands do lack nobility that they strike
A meaner than myself, since I myself
Have given myself the cause.
Enter the MESSENGER again

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Come hither, sir.
Though it be honest, it is never good
To bring bad news. Give to a gracious message
An host of tongues, but let ill tidings tell
Themselves when they be felt.

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: I have done my duty.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Is he married?
I cannot hate thee worser than I do
If thou again say “yes.”

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: He’s married, madam.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: The gods confound thee! Dost thou hold there still?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Should I lie, madam?

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Oh, I would thou didst,
So half my Egypt were submerged and made
A cistern for scaled snakes! Go, get thee hence.
Hadst thou Narcissus in thy face, to me
Thou wouldst appear most ugly. He is married?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: I crave your highness’ pardon.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: He is married?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text:
Take no offense that I would not offend you.
To punish me for what you make me do
Seems much unequal. He’s married to Octavia.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Oh, that his fault should make a knave of thee,
That art not what th’ art sure of! Get thee hence.
The merchandise which thou hast brought from Rome
Are all too dear for me. Lie they upon thy hand
And be undone by ’em!
Exit MESSENGER

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Good your highness, patience.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text: In praising Antony, I have dispraised Caesar.

units.speaker: CHARMIAN
units.text: Many times, madam.

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
I am paid for ’t now. Lead me from hence.
I faint. O Iras, Charmian! ’Tis no matter.—
Go to the fellow, good Alexas. Bid him
Report the feature of Octavia: her years,
Her inclination. Let him not leave out
The color of her hair. Bring me word quickly.
Exit Alexas

units.speaker: CLEOPATRA
units.text:
Let him for ever go:—let him not—Charmian,
Though he be painted one way like a Gorgon,
The other way's a Mars. Bid you Alexas
To MARDIAN
Bring me word how tall she is. Pity me, Charmian,
But do not speak to me. Lead me to my chamber.
Exeunt
Flourish. Enter POMPEY and MENAS at one door, with drum and trumpet; at another CAESAR, LEPIDUS, ANTONY, ENOBARBUS, MAECENAS, and AGRIPPA, with soldiers marching

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
Your hostages I have, so have you mine,
And we shall talk before we fight.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
Most meet
That first we come to words, and therefore have we
Our written purposes before us sent,
Which, if thou hast considered, let us know
If ’twill tie up thy discontented sword
And carry back to Sicily much tall youth
That else must perish here.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
To you all three,
The senators alone of this great world,
Chief factors for the gods: I do not know
Wherefore my father should revengers want,
Having a son and friends, since Julius Caesar,
Who at Philippi the good Brutus ghosted,
There saw you laboring for him. What was ’t
That moved pale Cassius to conspire? And what
Made the all-honored, honest Roman Brutus,
With the armed rest, courtiers of beauteous freedom,
To drench the Capitol, but that they would
Have one man but a man? And that is it
Hath made me rig my navy, at whose burden
The angered ocean foams, with which I meant
To scourge th’ ingratitude that despiteful Rome
Cast on my noble father.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: Take your time.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Thou canst not fear us, Pompey, with thy sails.
We’ll speak with thee at sea. At land, thou know’st
How much we do o’ercount thee.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
At land indeed
Thou dost o’ercount me of my father’s house,
But since the cuckoo builds not for himself,
Remain in ’t as thou mayst.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Be pleased to tell us—
For this is from the present—how you take
The offers we have sent you.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: There’s the point.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Which do not be entreated to, but weigh
What it is worth embraced.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
And what may follow,
To try a larger fortune.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
You have made me offer
Of Sicily, Sardinia. And I must
Rid all the sea of pirates, then, to send
Measures of wheat to Rome. This ’greed upon
To part with unhacked edges and bear back
Our targes undinted.

units.speaker: CAESAR ANTONY LEPIDUS
units.text: That’s our offer.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
Know then
I came before you here a man prepared
To take this offer, but Mark Antony
Put me to some impatience. [To ANTONY] —Though I lose
The praise of it by telling, you must know,
When Caesar and your brother were at blows,
Your mother came to Sicily and did find
Her welcome friendly.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
[To POMPEY] I have heard it, Pompey,
And am well studied for a liberal thanks
Which I do owe you.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: Let me have your hand.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
The beds i’ th’ East are soft, and thanks to you,
That called me timelier than my purpose hither,
For I have gained by ’t.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
[To POMPEY] Since I saw you last
There’s a change upon you.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
Well, I know not
What counts harsh Fortune casts upon my face,
But in my bosom shall she never come
To make my heart her vassal.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: Well met here.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
I hope so, Lepidus. Thus we are agreed.
I crave our composition may be written
And sealed between us.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: That’s the next to do.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
We’ll feast each other ere we part, and let’s
Draw lots who shall begin.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: That will I, Pompey.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
No, Antony, take the lot. But, first or last,
Your fine Egyptian cookery shall have
The fame. I have heard that Julius Caesar
Grew fat with feasting there.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: You have heard much.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: I have fair meanings, sir.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: And fair words to them.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
Then so much have I heard.
And I have heard Apollodorus carried—

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: [interrupting POMPEY] No more of that. He did so.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: What, I pray you?

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: A certain queen to Caesar in a mattress.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: I know thee now. How far’st thou, soldier?

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Well,
And well am like to do, for I perceive,
Four feasts are toward.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
Let me shake thy hand.
I never hated thee. I have seen thee fight
When I have envied thy behavior.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Sir,
I never loved you much, but I ha’ praised ye
When you have well deserved ten times as much
As I have said you did.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
Enjoy thy plainness.
It nothing ill becomes thee.—
Aboard my galley I invite you all.
Will you lead, lords?

units.speaker: CAESAR ANTONY LEPIDUS
units.text: Show ’s the way, sir.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: Come.
Exeunt all but ENOBARBUS and MENAS

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
(aside] Thy father, Pompey, would ne’er have made this
treaty. [To ENOBARBUS] You and I have known, sir.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: At sea, I think.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: We have, sir.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: You have done well by water.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: And you by land.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
I will praise any man that will praise me, though it
cannot be denied what I have done by land.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Nor what I have done by water.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Yes, something you can deny for your own safety: you
have been a great thief by sea.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: And you by land.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
There I deny my land service. But give me your hand,
Menas.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: All men’s faces are true, whatsome’er their hands are.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: But there is never a fair woman has a true face.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: No slander. They steal hearts.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: We came hither to fight with you.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
For my part, I am sorry it is turned to a drinking.
Pompey doth this day laugh away his fortune.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: If he do, sure he cannot weep ’t back again.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
You’ve said, sir. We looked not for Mark Antony here.
Pray you, is he married to Cleopatra?

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Caesar’s sister is called Octavia.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: True, sir. She was the wife of Caius Marcellus.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: But she is now the wife of Marcus Antonius.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Pray ye, sir?

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: ’Tis true.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Then is Caesar and he forever knit together.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
If I were bound to divine of this unity, I would not
prophesy so.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
I think the policy of that purpose made more in the
marriage than the love of the parties.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
I think so too. But you shall find the band that seems
to tie their friendship together will be the very
strangler of their amity. Octavia is of a holy, cold,
and still conversation.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Who would not have his wife so?

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Not he that himself is not so, which is Mark Antony. He
will to his Egyptian dish again. Then shall the sighs
of Octavia blow the fire up in Caesar, and, as I said
before, that which is the strength of their amity shall
prove the immediate author of their variance. Antony
will use his affection where it is. He married but his
occasion here.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
And thus it may be. Come, sir, will you aboard? I have
a health for you.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
I shall take it, sir. We have used our throats in
Egypt.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Come, let’s away.
Exeunt
Music plays. Enter two or three SERVANTS with a banquet

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text:
Here they’ll be, man. Some o’ their plants are
ill-rooted already. The least wind i’ th’ world will
blow them down.

units.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
units.text: Lepidus is high-colored.

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text: They have made him drink alms-drink.

units.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
units.text:
As they pinch one another by the disposition, he cries
out, “No more,” reconciles them to his entreaty and
himself to th’ drink.

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text:
But it raises the greater war between him and his
discretion.

units.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
units.text:
Why, this it is to have a name in great men’s
fellowship. I had as lief have a reed that will do me no
service as a partisan I could not heave.

units.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
units.text:
To be called into a huge sphere, and not to be seen to
move in ’t, are the holes where eyes should be, which
pitifully disaster the cheeks.
A sennet sounded. Enter CAESAR, ANTONY, POMPEY, LEPIDUS, AGRIPPA, MAECENAS, ENOBARBUS, and MENAS, with other captains and a BOY

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Thus do they, sir: they take the flow o’ th’ Nile
By certain scales i’ th’ Pyramid. They know
By th’ height, the lowness, or the mean, if dearth
Or foison follow. The higher Nilus swells
The more it promises. As it ebbs, the seedsman
Upon the slime and ooze scatters his grain,
And shortly comes to harvest.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: You’ve strange serpents there?

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Ay, Lepidus.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Your serpent of Egypt is bred now of your mud by the
operation of your sun. So is your crocodile.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: They are so.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: [To LEPIDUS] Sit, and some wine. A health to Lepidus!

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: I am not so well as I should be, but I’ll ne’er out.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
Not till you have slept. I fear me you’ll be in till
then.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text:
Nay, certainly, I have heard the Ptolemies’ pyramises
are very goodly things. Without contradiction I have
heard that.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: [aside to POMPEY] Pompey, a word.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: [aside to MENAS] Say in mine ear. What is ’t?

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
[aside to POMPEY] Forsake thy seat, I do beseech thee,
captain,
And hear me speak a word.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
[aside to MENAS] Forbear me till anon.—This wine for
Lepidus!

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: What manner o’ thing is your crocodile?

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
It is shaped, sir, like itself, and it is as broad as
it hath breadth. It is just so high as it is, and moves
with its own organs. It lives by that which nourisheth
it, and, the elements once out of it, it transmigrates.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: What color is it of?

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Of it own color too.

units.speaker: LEPIDUS
units.text: ’Tis a strange serpent.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: ’Tis so. And the tears of it are wet.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: [aside to ANTONY] Will this description satisfy him?

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
[aside to CAESAR] With the health that Pompey gives
him, else he is a very epicure.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
[aside to MENAS] Go hang, sir, hang! Tell me of that?
Away!
Do as I bid you.—Where’s this cup I called for?

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
[aside to POMPEY] If for the sake of merit thou wilt
hear me,
Rise from thy stool.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: [aside to MENAS] I think th’ art mad.
He rises, and they walk aside

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: The matter?

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: I have ever held my cap off to thy fortunes.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
Thou hast served me with much faith. What’s else to
say?—
[To the others] Be jolly, lords.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
These quicksands, Lepidus,
Keep off them, for you sink.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: [aside to POMPEY] Wilt thou be lord of all the world?

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: What sayst thou?

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Wilt thou be lord of the whole world? That’s twice.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: How should that be?

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
But entertain it,
And, though thou think me poor, I am the man
Will give thee all the world.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: Hast thou drunk well?

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
No, Pompey, I have kept me from the cup.
Thou art, if thou dar’st be, the earthly Jove.
Whate’er the ocean pales or sky inclips
Is thine, if thou wilt ha ’t.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: Show me which way.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
These three world-sharers, these competitors,
Are in thy vessel. Let me cut the cable,
And, when we are put off, fall to their throats.
All there is thine.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
Ah, this thou shouldst have done
And not have spoke on ’t! In me ’tis villainy,
In thee ’t had been good service. Thou must know,
’Tis not my profit that does lead mine honor;
Mine honor, it. Repent that e’er thy tongue
Hath so betrayed thine act. Being done unknown,
I should have found it afterwards well done,
But must condemn it now. Desist, and drink.
He returns to the feast

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
[aside] For this,
I’ll never follow thy palled fortunes more.
Who seeks and will not take when once ’tis offered
Shall never find it more.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: This health to Lepidus!

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
[To a servant] Bear him ashore.—I’ll pledge it for him,
Pompey.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Here’s to thee, Menas!

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Enobarbus, welcome.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: Fill till the cup be hid.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: There’s a strong fellow, Menas.
Pointing to the servant who carries off LEPIDUS

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Why?

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
He bears
The third part of the world, man. Seest not?

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
The third part, then, is drunk. Would it were all,
That it might go on wheels!

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Drink thou. Increase the reels.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Come.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: This is not yet an Alexandrian feast.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
It ripens towards it. Strike the vessels, ho!
Here’s to Caesar.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
I could well forbear ’t.
It’s monstrous labor when I wash my brain
And it grows fouler.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: Be a child o’ th’ time.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
Possess it, I’ll make answer.
But I had rather fast from all four days
Than drink so much in one.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
[To ANTONY] Ha! My brave emperor,
Shall we dance now the Egyptian bacchanals
And celebrate our drink?

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: Let’s ha ’t, good soldier.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Come, let’s all take hands
Till that the conquering wine hath steeped our sense
In soft and delicate Lethe.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
All take hands.
Make battery to our ears with the loud music,
The while I’ll place you; then the boy shall sing.
The holding every man shall beat as loud
As his strong sides can volley.
Music plays. ENOBARBUS places them hand in hand

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: The Song.

units.speaker: BOY
units.text:
(Sings) Come, thou monarch of the vine,
Plumpy Bacchus with pink eyne!
In thy vats our cares be drowned,
With thy grapes our hairs be crowned.

units.speaker: ALL
units.text:
(Singing) Cup us till the world go round,
Cup us till the world go round!

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
What would you more? —Pompey, good night. [To ANTONY]
Good brother,
Let me request you off. Our graver business
Frowns at this levity. —Gentle lords, let’s part.
You see we have burnt our cheeks. Strong Enobarb
Is weaker than the wine; and mine own tongue
Splits what it speaks. The wild disguise hath almost
Anticked us all. What needs more words? Good night.
Good Antony, your hand.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text: I’ll try you on the shore.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: And shall, sir. Give ’s your hand.

units.speaker: POMPEY
units.text:
O Antony, You have my father’s house.
But what? We are friends. Come, down into the boat.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Take heed you fall not.
Exeunt all but MENAS and ENOBARBUS

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Menas, I’ll not on shore.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text:
No, to my cabin. These drums, these trumpets, flutes!
What!
Let Neptune hear we bid a loud farewell
To these great fellows. Sound and be hanged, sound out!
Sound a flourish, with drums

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Hoo! says ’a. There’s my cap.

units.speaker: MENAS
units.text: Hoo! Noble captain, come.
Exeunt
Enter VENTIDIUS as it were in triumph, the dead body of Pacorus borne before him, with SILIUS, and other Romans, officers, and soldiers

units.speaker: VENTIDIUS
units.text:
Now, darting Parthia, art thou struck, and now
Pleased fortune does of Marcus Crassus’ death
Make me revenger. Bear the King’s son’s body
Before our army. Thy Pacorus, Orodes,
Pays this for Marcus Crassus.

units.speaker: SILIUS
units.text:
Noble Ventidius,
Whilst yet with Parthian blood thy sword is warm,
The fugitive Parthians follow. Spur through Media,
Mesopotamia, and the shelters whither
The routed fly. So thy grand captain, Antony,
Shall set thee on triumphant chariots and
Put garlands on thy head.

units.speaker: VENTIDIUS
units.text:
O Silius, Silius,
I have done enough. A lower place, note well,
May make too great an act. For learn this, Silius:
Better to leave undone than by our deed
Acquire too high a fame when him we serve’s away.
Caesar and Antony have ever won
More in their officer than person. Sossius,
One of my place in Syria, his lieutenant,
For quick accumulation of renown,
Which he achieved by th’ minute, lost his favor.
Who does i’ th’ wars more than his captain can
Becomes his captain’s captain; and ambition,
The soldier’s virtue, rather makes choice of loss
Than gain which darkens him.
I could do more to do Antonius good,
But ’twould offend him, and in his offense
Should my performance perish.

units.speaker: SILIUS
units.text:
Thou hast, Ventidius, that
Without the which a soldier and his sword
Grants scarce distinction. Thou wilt write to Antony?

units.speaker: VENTIDIUS
units.text:
I’ll humbly signify what in his name,
That magical word of war, we have effected:
How with his banners and his well-paid ranks
The ne’er-yet-beaten horse of Parthia
We have jaded out o’ th’ field.

units.speaker: SILIUS
units.text: Where is he now?

units.speaker: VENTIDIUS
units.text:
He purposeth to Athens, whither, with what haste
The weight we must convey with’s will permit,
We shall appear before him. [To the soliders] On,
there. Pass along!
Exeunt
Enter AGRIPPA at one door, ENOBARBUS at another

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: What, are the brothers parted?

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
They have dispatched with Pompey; he is gone.
The other three are sealing. Octavia weeps
To part from Rome. Caesar is sad, and Lepidus,
Since Pompey’s feast, as Menas says, is troubled
With the greensickness.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: ’Tis a noble Lepidus.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: A very fine one. Oh, how he loves Caesar!

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: Nay, but how dearly he adores Mark Antony!

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Caesar? Why, he’s the Jupiter of men.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: What’s Antony? The god of Jupiter.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Spake you of Caesar? How, the nonpareil!

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: O Antony, O thou Arabian bird!

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: Would you praise Caesar, say “Caesar.” Go no further.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: Indeed, he plied them both with excellent praises.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
But he loves Caesar best; yet he loves Antony.
Hoo! Hearts, tongues, figures, scribes, bards, poets,
cannot
Think, speak, cast, write, sing, number—hoo!—
His love to Antony. But as for Caesar,
Kneel down, kneel down, and wonder.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: Both he loves.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: They are his shards, and he their beetle.
Trumpets within

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text:
So,
This is to horse. Adieu, noble Agrippa.

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: Good fortune, worthy soldier, and farewell.
Enter CAESAR, ANTONY, LEPIDUS, and OCTAVIA

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text: No further, sir.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
You take from me a great part of myself;
Use me well in ’t. —Sister, prove such a wife
As my thoughts make thee, and as my farthest bond
Shall pass on thy approof. —Most noble Antony,
Let not the piece of virtue, which is set
Betwixt us as the cement of our love,
To keep it builded, be the ram to batter
The fortress of it. For better might we
Have loved without this mean, if on both parts
This be not cherished.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Make me not offended
In your distrust.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: I have said.

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
You shall not find,
Though you be therein curious, the least cause
For what you seem to fear. So the gods keep you
And make the hearts of Romans serve your ends.
We will here part.

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text:
Farewell, my dearest sister, fare thee well.
The elements be kind to thee and make
Thy spirits all of comfort! Fare thee well.

units.speaker: OCTAVIA
units.text: My noble brother!

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
The April’s in her eyes; it is love’s spring,
And these the showers to bring it on. [To OCTAVIA] Be
cheerful.

units.speaker: OCTAVIA
units.text: [To CAESAR] Sir, look well to my husband’s house, and—

units.speaker: CAESAR
units.text: What, Octavia?

units.speaker: OCTAVIA
units.text: I’ll tell you in your ear.
She and CAESAR walk aside

units.speaker: ANTONY
units.text:
Her tongue will not obey her heart, nor can
Her heart inform her tongue —the swan’s-down feather
That stands upon the swell at the full of tide
And neither way inclines.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
units.text: [aside to AGRIPPA] Will Caesar weep?

units.speaker: AGRIPPA
units.text: [aside to ENOBARBUS] He has a cloud in ’s face.

units.speaker: ENOBARBUS
AGRIPPA
units.text:
[aside to ENOBARBUS] Why, Enobarbus,
When Antony found Julius Caesar dead,
He cried almost to roaring, and he wept
When at Philippi he found Brutus slain.
