sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
With most gladness,
And do invite you to my sister’s view,
Whither straight I’ll lead you.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
With most gladness,
And do invite you to my sister’s view,
Whither straight I’ll lead you.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: Let us, Lepidus, not lack your company.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text: Noble Antony, not sickness should detain me.
Flourish. Exeunt all but ENOBARBUS, AGRIPPA, and MAECENAS
Flourish. Exeunt all but ENOBARBUS, AGRIPPA, and MAECENAS

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text: [To ENOBARBUS] Welcome from Egypt, sir.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Half the heart of Caesar, worthy Maecenas! My honorable
friend, Agrippa.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Half the heart of Caesar, worthy Maecenas! My honorable
friend, Agrippa.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Good Enobarbus!

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text:
We have cause to be glad that matters are so well
digested.
You stayed well by ’t in Egypt.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text:
We have cause to be glad that matters are so well
digested.
You stayed well by ’t in Egypt.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Ay, sir, we did sleep day out of countenance and made
the night light with drinking.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text:
Eight wild boars roasted whole at a breakfast—and but
twelve persons there! Is this true?

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text:
Eight wild boars roasted whole at a breakfast—and but
twelve persons there! Is this true?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
This was but as a fly by an eagle. We had much more
monstrous matter of feast, which worthily deserved
noting.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
This was but as a fly by an eagle. We had much more
monstrous matter of feast, which worthily deserved
noting.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text:
She’s a most triumphant lady, if report be square to
her.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
When she first met Mark Antony, she pursed up his heart
upon the river of Cydnus.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text:
There she appeared indeed, or my reporter devised well
for her.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
I will tell you.
The barge she sat in, like a burnished throne,
Burned on the water. The poop was beaten gold,
Purple the sails, and so perfumèd that
The winds were lovesick with them. The oars were
silver,
Which to the tune of flutes kept stroke, and made
The water which they beat to follow faster,
As amorous of their strokes. For her own person,
It beggared all description: she did lie
In her pavilion—cloth-of-gold, of tissue—
O’erpicturing that Venus where we see
The fancy outwork nature. On each side her
Stood pretty dimpled boys, like smiling Cupids,
With divers-colored fans, whose wind did seem
To glow the delicate cheeks which they did cool,
And what they undid did.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
I will tell you.
The barge she sat in, like a burnished throne,
Burned on the water. The poop was beaten gold,
Purple the sails, and so perfumèd that
The winds were lovesick with them. The oars were
silver,
Which to the tune of flutes kept stroke, and made
The water which they beat to follow faster,
As amorous of their strokes. For her own person,
It beggared all description: she did lie
In her pavilion—cloth-of-gold, of tissue—
O’erpicturing that Venus where we see
The fancy outwork nature. On each side her
Stood pretty dimpled boys, like smiling Cupids,
With divers-colored fans, whose wind did seem
To glow the delicate cheeks which they did cool,
And what they undid did.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Oh, rare for Antony!

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Her gentlewomen, like the Nereides,
So many mermaids, tended her i’ th’ eyes,
And made their bends adornings. At the helm
A seeming mermaid steers. The silken tackle
Swell with the touches of those flower-soft hands
That yarely frame the office. From the barge
A strange invisible perfume hits the sense
Of the adjacent wharfs. The city cast
Her people out upon her, and Antony,
Enthroned i’ th’ marketplace, did sit alone,
Whistling to th’ air, which, but for vacancy,
Had gone to gaze on Cleopatra too
And made a gap in nature.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Rare Egyptian!

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Upon her landing, Antony sent to her,
Invited her to supper. She replied
It should be better he became her guest,
Which she entreated. Our courteous Antony,
Whom ne’er the word of “No” woman heard speak,
Being barbered ten times o’er, goes to the feast,
And for his ordinary pays his heart
For what his eyes eat only.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text:
Royal wench!
She made great Caesar lay his sword to bed.
He plowed her, and she cropped.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text:

She made great Caesar lay his sword to bed.
He plowed her, and she cropped.

Royal wench!

He plowed her, and she cropped.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
I saw her once
Hop forty paces through the public street,
And having lost her breath, she spoke, and panted,
That she did make defect perfection,
And, breathless, pour breathe forth.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text: Now Antony must leave her utterly.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Never. He will not.
Age cannot wither her, nor custom stale
Her infinite variety. Other women cloy
The appetites they feed, but she makes hungry
Where most she satisfies, for vilest things
Become themselves in her, that the holy priests
Bless her when she is riggish.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text:
If beauty, wisdom, modesty, can settle
The heart of Antony, Octavia is
A blessèd lottery to him.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text:
Let us go.
Good Enobarbus, make yourself my guest
Whilst you abide here.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text:
Let us go.
Good Enobarbus, make yourself my guest
Whilst you abide here.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: Humbly, sir, I thank you.
Exeunt
Enter ANTONY, CAESAR; OCTAVIA between them

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
[To OCTAVIA] The world and my great office will
sometimes
Divide me from your bosom.

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text:
All which time
Before the gods my knee shall bow my prayers
To them for you.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
[To CAESAR] Good night, sir. —My Octavia,
Read not my blemishes in the world’s report.
I have not kept my square, but that to come
Shall all be done by th’ rule. Good night, dear lady.
[To CAESAR] Good night, sir.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: Good night.
He exits with OCTAVIA
Enter SOOTHSAYER

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: Now, sirrah, you do wish yourself in Egypt?

sentences.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
sentences.text: Would I had never come from thence, nor you thither.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: If you can, your reason?

sentences.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
sentences.text:
I see it in my motion, have it not in my tongue. But
yet hie you to Egypt again.

sentences.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
sentences.text:
I see it in my motion, have it not in my tongue. But
yet hie you to Egypt again.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Say to me whose fortunes shall rise higher,
Caesar’s or mine?

sentences.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
sentences.text:
Caesar’s.
Therefore, O Antony, stay not by his side.
Thy dæmon—that thy spirit which keeps thee—is
Noble, courageous, high, unmatchable
Where Caesar’s is not. But near him thy angel
Becomes afeard, as being o’erpowered. Therefore
Make space enough between you.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: Speak this no more.

sentences.speaker: SOOTHSAYER
sentences.text:
To none but thee, no more but when to thee.
If thou dost play with him at any game,
Thou art sure to lose, and of that natural luck
He beats thee ’gainst the odds. Thy luster thickens
When he shines by. I say again, thy spirit
Is all afraid to govern thee near him,
But, he away, ’tis noble.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Get thee gone.
Say to Ventidius I would speak with him.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Get thee gone.
Say to Ventidius I would speak with him.
Exit SOOTHSAYER

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
[To himself] He shall to Parthia. Be it art or hap,
He hath spoken true. The very dice obey him,
And in our sports my better cunning faints
Under his chance. If we draw lots, he speeds.
His cocks do win the battle still of mine
When it is all to naught, and his quails ever
Beat mine, inhooped, at odds. I will to Egypt.
And though I make this marriage for my peace,
I’ th’ East my pleasure lies.

Enter VENTIDIUS

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
O come, Ventidius.
You must to Parthia. Your commission’s ready.
Follow me and receive ’t.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
O come, Ventidius.
You must to Parthia. Your commission’s ready.
Follow me and receive ’t.

Exeunt
Enter LEPIDUS, MECAENAS, and AGRIPPA

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Trouble yourselves no further. Pray you, hasten
Your generals after.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Trouble yourselves no further. Pray you, hasten
Your generals after.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text:
Sir, Mark Antony
Will e’en but kiss Octavia, and we’ll follow.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Till I shall see you in your soldiers’ dress,
Which will become you both, farewell.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text:
We shall,
As I conceive the journey, be at the Mount
Before you, Lepidus.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Your way is shorter.
My purposes do draw me much about.
You’ll win two days upon me.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Your way is shorter.
My purposes do draw me much about.
You’ll win two days upon me.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Your way is shorter.
My purposes do draw me much about.
You’ll win two days upon me.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Sir, good success.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text: Farewell.
Exeunt
Enter CLEOPATRA, CHARMIAN, IRAS, and ALEXAS

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Give me some music. Music, moody food
Of us that trade in love.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Give me some music. Music, moody food
Of us that trade in love.

sentences.speaker: ALL
sentences.text: The music, ho!
Enter MARDIAN the eunuch

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Let it alone. Let’s to billiards. Come, Charmian.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Let it alone. Let’s to billiards. Come, Charmian.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Let it alone. Let’s to billiards. Come, Charmian.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text: My arm is sore. Best play with Mardian.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text: My arm is sore. Best play with Mardian.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
As well a woman with an eunuch played
As with a woman.—Come, you’ll play with me, sir?

sentences.speaker: MARDIAN
sentences.text: As well as I can, madam.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
And when good will is showed, though ’t come too short,
The actor may plead pardon. I’ll none now.
Give me mine angle. We’ll to th’ river. There,
My music playing far off, I will betray
Tawny-finned fishes. My bended hook shall pierce
Their slimy jaws, and as I draw them up
I’ll think them every one an Antony
And say, “Aha! You’re caught.”

Their slimy jaws, and as I draw them up
I’ll think them every one an Antony
And say, “Aha! You’re caught.”

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text:
’Twas merry when
You wagered on your angling, when your diver
Did hang a salt fish on his hook, which he
With fervency drew up.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
That time—Oh, times!—
I laughed him out of patience, and that night
I laughed him into patience. And next morn,
Ere the ninth hour, I drunk him to his bed,
Then put my tires and mantles on him, whilst
I wore his sword Philippan.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:

I wore his sword Philippan.
Enter a MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Oh, from Italy!
Ram thou thy fruitful tidings in mine ears,
That long time have been barren.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Oh, from Italy!
Ram thou thy fruitful tidings in mine ears,
That long time have been barren.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Madam, madam—

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Antonio’s dead! If thou say so, villain,
Thou kill’st thy mistress. But well and free,
If thou so yield him, there is gold, and here
My bluest veins to kiss —a hand that kings
Have lipped, and trembled kissing.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:

Thou kill’st thy mistress. But well and free,
If thou so yield him, there is gold, and here
My bluest veins to kiss —a hand that kings
Have lipped, and trembled kissing.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: First, madam, he is well.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Why, there’s more gold. But, sirrah, mark, we use
To say the dead are well. Bring it to that,
The gold I give thee will I melt and pour
Down thy ill-uttering throat.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:

To say the dead are well. Bring it to that,
The gold I give thee will I melt and pour
Down thy ill-uttering throat.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Good madam, hear me.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Well, go to, I will.
But there’s no goodness in thy face—if Antony
Be free and healthful, so tart a favor
To trumpet such good tidings! If not well,
Thou shouldst come like a Fury crowned with snakes,
Not like a formal man.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Will ’t please you hear me?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
I have a mind to strike thee ere thou speak’st.
Yet if thou say Antony lives, is well,
Or friends with Caesar, or not captive to him,
I’ll set thee in a shower of gold and hail
Rich pearls upon thee.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Madam, he’s well.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Well said.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: And friends with Caesar.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Th’ art an honest man.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Caesar and he are greater friends than ever.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Make thee a fortune from me.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: But yet, madam—

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
I do not like “But yet.” It does allay
The good precedence. Fie upon “But yet.”
“But yet” is as a jailer to bring forth
Some monstrous malefactor. Prithee, friend,
Pour out the pack of matter to mine ear,
The good and bad together. He’s friends with Caesar,
In state of health, thou say’st, and, thou say’st,
free.

sentences.text:
Free, madam, no. I made no such report.
He’s bound unto Octavia.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: For what good turn?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: For the best turn i’ th’ bed.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: I am pale, Charmian.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Madam, he’s married to Octavia.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: The most infectious pestilence upon thee!

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: The most infectious pestilence upon thee!

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Good madam, patience.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: What say you?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: What say you?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Gracious madam,
I that do bring the news made not the match.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Say ’tis not so, a province I will give thee
And make thy fortunes proud. The blow thou hadst
Shall make thy peace for moving me to rage,
And I will boot thee with what gift beside
Thy modesty can beg.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: He’s married, madam.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Rogue, thou hast lived too long.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Rogue, thou hast lived too long.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Nay then, I’ll run.
What mean you, madam? I have made no fault.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text:
Good madam, keep yourself within yourself.
The man is innocent.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text:
Good madam, keep yourself within yourself.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Some innocents ’scape not the thunderbolt.
Melt Egypt into Nile, and kindly creatures
Turn all to serpents. Call the slave again.
Though I am mad, I will not bite him. Call!

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
I will not hurt him.
These hands do lack nobility that they strike
A meaner than myself, since I myself
Have given myself the cause.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Come hither, sir.
Though it be honest, it is never good
To bring bad news. Give to a gracious message
An host of tongues, but let ill tidings tell
Themselves when they be felt.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: I have done my duty.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Is he married?
I cannot hate thee worser than I do
If thou again say “yes.”

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: He’s married, madam.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: The gods confound thee! Dost thou hold there still?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: The gods confound thee! Dost thou hold there still?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Should I lie, madam?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Oh, I would thou didst,
So half my Egypt were submerged and made
A cistern for scaled snakes! Go, get thee hence.
Hadst thou Narcissus in thy face, to me
Thou wouldst appear most ugly. He is married?

sentences.text: I crave your highness’ pardon.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: He is married?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Take no offense that I would not offend you.
To punish me for what you make me do
Seems much unequal. He’s married to Octavia.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Oh, that his fault should make a knave of thee,
That art not what th’ art sure of! Get thee hence.
The merchandise which thou hast brought from Rome
Are all too dear for me. Lie they upon thy hand
And be undone by ’em!

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text: Good your highness, patience.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: In praising Antony, I have dispraised Caesar.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text: Many times, madam.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
I am paid for ’t now. Lead me from hence.
I faint. O Iras, Charmian! ’Tis no matter.—
Go to the fellow, good Alexas. Bid him
Report the feature of Octavia: her years,
Her inclination. Let him not leave out
The color of her hair. Bring me word quickly.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Let him for ever go:—let him not—Charmian,
Though he be painted one way like a Gorgon,
The other way's a Mars. Bid you Alexas
To MARDIAN
Bring me word how tall she is. Pity me, Charmian,
But do not speak to me. Lead me to my chamber.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Your hostages I have, so have you mine,
And we shall talk before we fight.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Most meet
That first we come to words, and therefore have we
Our written purposes before us sent,
Which, if thou hast considered, let us know
If ’twill tie up thy discontented sword
And carry back to Sicily much tall youth
That else must perish here.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Most meet
That first we come to words, and therefore have we
Our written purposes before us sent,
Which, if thou hast considered, let us know
If ’twill tie up thy discontented sword
And carry back to Sicily much tall youth
That else must perish here.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
To you all three,
The senators alone of this great world,
Chief factors for the gods: I do not know
Wherefore my father should revengers want,
Having a son and friends, since Julius Caesar,
Who at Philippi the good Brutus ghosted,
There saw you laboring for him. What was ’t
That moved pale Cassius to conspire? And what
Made the all-honored, honest Roman Brutus,
With the armed rest, courtiers of beauteous freedom,
To drench the Capitol, but that they would
Have one man but a man? And that is it
Hath made me rig my navy, at whose burden
The angered ocean foams, with which I meant
To scourge th’ ingratitude that despiteful Rome
Cast on my noble father.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: Take your time.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Thou canst not fear us, Pompey, with thy sails.
We’ll speak with thee at sea. At land, thou know’st
How much we do o’ercount thee.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:

We’ll speak with thee at sea. At land, thou know’st
How much we do o’ercount thee.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
At land indeed
Thou dost o’ercount me of my father’s house,
But since the cuckoo builds not for himself,
Remain in ’t as thou mayst.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
At land indeed
Thou dost o’ercount me of my father’s house,
But since the cuckoo builds not for himself,
Remain in ’t as thou mayst.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Be pleased to tell us—
For this is from the present—how you take
The offers we have sent you.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: There’s the point.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Which do not be entreated to, but weigh
What it is worth embraced.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
And what may follow,
To try a larger fortune.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
You have made me offer
Of Sicily, Sardinia. And I must
Rid all the sea of pirates, then, to send
Measures of wheat to Rome. This ’greed upon
To part with unhacked edges and bear back
Our targes undinted.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
You have made me offer
Of Sicily, Sardinia. And I must
Rid all the sea of pirates, then, to send
Measures of wheat to Rome. This ’greed upon
To part with unhacked edges and bear back
Our targes undinted.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
You have made me offer
Of Sicily, Sardinia. And I must
Rid all the sea of pirates, then, to send
Measures of wheat to Rome. This ’greed upon
To part with unhacked edges and bear back
Our targes undinted.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR ANTONY LEPIDUS
sentences.text: That’s our offer.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Know then
I came before you here a man prepared
To take this offer, but Mark Antony
Put me to some impatience. [To ANTONY] —Though I lose
The praise of it by telling, you must know,
When Caesar and your brother were at blows,
Your mother came to Sicily and did find
Her welcome friendly.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
[To POMPEY] I have heard it, Pompey,
And am well studied for a liberal thanks
Which I do owe you.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: Let me have your hand.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: Let me have your hand.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: Let me have your hand.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: Let me have your hand.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
The beds i’ th’ East are soft, and thanks to you,
That called me timelier than my purpose hither,
For I have gained by ’t.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
[To POMPEY] Since I saw you last
There’s a change upon you.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Well, I know not
What counts harsh Fortune casts upon my face,
But in my bosom shall she never come
To make my heart her vassal.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text: Well met here.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
I hope so, Lepidus. Thus we are agreed.
I crave our composition may be written
And sealed between us.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
I hope so, Lepidus. Thus we are agreed.
I crave our composition may be written
And sealed between us.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
I hope so, Lepidus. Thus we are agreed.
I crave our composition may be written
And sealed between us.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: That’s the next to do.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
We’ll feast each other ere we part, and let’s
Draw lots who shall begin.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: That will I, Pompey.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
No, Antony, take the lot. But, first or last,
Your fine Egyptian cookery shall have
The fame. I have heard that Julius Caesar
Grew fat with feasting there.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
No, Antony, take the lot. But, first or last,
Your fine Egyptian cookery shall have
The fame. I have heard that Julius Caesar
Grew fat with feasting there.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
No, Antony, take the lot. But, first or last,
Your fine Egyptian cookery shall have
The fame. I have heard that Julius Caesar
Grew fat with feasting there.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: You have heard much.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: I have fair meanings, sir.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: And fair words to them.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Then so much have I heard.
And I have heard Apollodorus carried—

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Then so much have I heard.
And I have heard Apollodorus carried—

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: [interrupting POMPEY] No more of that. He did so.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: [interrupting POMPEY] No more of that. He did so.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: What, I pray you?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: A certain queen to Caesar in a mattress.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: I know thee now. How far’st thou, soldier?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: I know thee now. How far’st thou, soldier?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Well,
And well am like to do, for I perceive,
Four feasts are toward.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Let me shake thy hand.
I never hated thee. I have seen thee fight
When I have envied thy behavior.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Let me shake thy hand.
I never hated thee. I have seen thee fight
When I have envied thy behavior.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Let me shake thy hand.
I never hated thee. I have seen thee fight
When I have envied thy behavior.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Sir,
I never loved you much, but I ha’ praised ye
When you have well deserved ten times as much
As I have said you did.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Enjoy thy plainness.
It nothing ill becomes thee.—
Aboard my galley I invite you all.
Will you lead, lords?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Enjoy thy plainness.
It nothing ill becomes thee.—
Aboard my galley I invite you all.
Will you lead, lords?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Enjoy thy plainness.
It nothing ill becomes thee.—
Aboard my galley I invite you all.
Will you lead, lords?

sentences.speaker: CAESAR ANTONY LEPIDUS
sentences.text: Show ’s the way, sir.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: Come.
Exeunt all but ENOBARBUS and MENAS

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
(aside] Thy father, Pompey, would ne’er have made this
treaty. [To ENOBARBUS] You and I have known, sir.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: At sea, I think.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: We have, sir.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: You have done well by water.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: And you by land.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
I will praise any man that will praise me, though it
cannot be denied what I have done by land.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: Nor what I have done by water.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Yes, something you can deny for your own safety: you
have been a great thief by sea.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: And you by land.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
There I deny my land service. But give me your hand,
Menas.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
There I deny my land service. But give me your hand,
Menas.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
There I deny my land service. But give me your hand,
Menas.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
There I deny my land service. But give me your hand,
Menas.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
There I deny my land service. But give me your hand,
Menas.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: All men’s faces are true, whatsome’er their hands are.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: But there is never a fair woman has a true face.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: No slander. They steal hearts.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: No slander. They steal hearts.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: We came hither to fight with you.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
For my part, I am sorry it is turned to a drinking.
Pompey doth this day laugh away his fortune.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
For my part, I am sorry it is turned to a drinking.
Pompey doth this day laugh away his fortune.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: If he do, sure he cannot weep ’t back again.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
You’ve said, sir. We looked not for Mark Antony here.
Pray you, is he married to Cleopatra?

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
You’ve said, sir. We looked not for Mark Antony here.
Pray you, is he married to Cleopatra?

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
You’ve said, sir. We looked not for Mark Antony here.
Pray you, is he married to Cleopatra?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: Caesar’s sister is called Octavia.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: True, sir. She was the wife of Caius Marcellus.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: True, sir. She was the wife of Caius Marcellus.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: But she is now the wife of Marcus Antonius.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: Pray ye, sir?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: ’Tis true.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: Then is Caesar and he forever knit together.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
If I were bound to divine of this unity, I would not
prophesy so.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
I think the policy of that purpose made more in the
marriage than the love of the parties.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
I think so too. But you shall find the band that seems
to tie their friendship together will be the very
strangler of their amity. Octavia is of a holy, cold,
and still conversation.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
I think so too. But you shall find the band that seems
to tie their friendship together will be the very
strangler of their amity. Octavia is of a holy, cold,
and still conversation.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
I think so too. But you shall find the band that seems
to tie their friendship together will be the very
strangler of their amity. Octavia is of a holy, cold,
and still conversation.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: Who would not have his wife so?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Not he that himself is not so, which is Mark Antony. He
will to his Egyptian dish again. Then shall the sighs
of Octavia blow the fire up in Caesar, and, as I said
before, that which is the strength of their amity shall
prove the immediate author of their variance. Antony
will use his affection where it is. He married but his
occasion here.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
And thus it may be. Come, sir, will you aboard? I have
a health for you.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
And thus it may be. Come, sir, will you aboard? I have
a health for you.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
And thus it may be. Come, sir, will you aboard? I have
a health for you.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
I shall take it, sir. We have used our throats in
Egypt.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
I shall take it, sir. We have used our throats in
Egypt.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: Come, let’s away.
Exeunt
Music plays. Enter two or three SERVANTS with a banquet
Music plays. Enter two or three SERVANTS with a banquet

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
Here they’ll be, man. Some o’ their plants are
ill-rooted already. The least wind i’ th’ world will
blow them down.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
Here they’ll be, man. Some o’ their plants are
ill-rooted already. The least wind i’ th’ world will
blow them down.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
Here they’ll be, man. Some o’ their plants are
ill-rooted already. The least wind i’ th’ world will
blow them down.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text: Lepidus is high-colored.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text: They have made him drink alms-drink.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text:
As they pinch one another by the disposition, he cries
out, “No more,” reconciles them to his entreaty and
himself to th’ drink.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
But it raises the greater war between him and his
discretion.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text:
Why, this it is to have a name in great men’s
fellowship. I had as lief have a reed that will do me no
service as a partisan I could not heave.

sentences.speaker: SECOND SERVANT
sentences.text:
Why, this it is to have a name in great men’s
fellowship. I had as lief have a reed that will do me no
service as a partisan I could not heave.

sentences.speaker: FIRST SERVANT
sentences.text:
To be called into a huge sphere, and not to be seen to
move in ’t, are the holes where eyes should be, which
pitifully disaster the cheeks.
A sennet sounded. Enter CAESAR, ANTONY, POMPEY, LEPIDUS, AGRIPPA, MAECENAS, ENOBARBUS, and MENAS, with other captains and a BOY
A sennet sounded. Enter CAESAR, ANTONY, POMPEY, LEPIDUS, AGRIPPA, MAECENAS, ENOBARBUS, and MENAS, with other captains and a BOY

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Thus do they, sir: they take the flow o’ th’ Nile
By certain scales i’ th’ Pyramid. They know
By th’ height, the lowness, or the mean, if dearth
Or foison follow. The higher Nilus swells
The more it promises. As it ebbs, the seedsman
Upon the slime and ooze scatters his grain,
And shortly comes to harvest.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text: You’ve strange serpents there?

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: Ay, Lepidus.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Your serpent of Egypt is bred now of your mud by the
operation of your sun. So is your crocodile.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Your serpent of Egypt is bred now of your mud by the
operation of your sun. So is your crocodile.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: They are so.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: [To LEPIDUS] Sit, and some wine. A health to Lepidus!

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: [To LEPIDUS] Sit, and some wine. A health to Lepidus!

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text: I am not so well as I should be, but I’ll ne’er out.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Not till you have slept. I fear me you’ll be in till
then.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Not till you have slept. I fear me you’ll be in till
then.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Nay, certainly, I have heard the Ptolemies’ pyramises
are very goodly things. Without contradiction I have
heard that.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
Nay, certainly, I have heard the Ptolemies’ pyramises
are very goodly things. Without contradiction I have
heard that.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: [aside to POMPEY] Pompey, a word.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: [aside to MENAS] Say in mine ear. What is ’t?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: [aside to MENAS] Say in mine ear. What is ’t?

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
[aside to POMPEY] Forsake thy seat, I do beseech thee,
captain,
And hear me speak a word.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
[aside to MENAS] Forbear me till anon.—This wine for
Lepidus!

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text: What manner o’ thing is your crocodile?

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
It is shaped, sir, like itself, and it is as broad as
it hath breadth. It is just so high as it is, and moves
with its own organs. It lives by that which nourisheth
it, and, the elements once out of it, it transmigrates.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
It is shaped, sir, like itself, and it is as broad as
it hath breadth. It is just so high as it is, and moves
with its own organs. It lives by that which nourisheth
it, and, the elements once out of it, it transmigrates.

sentences.text:
It is shaped, sir, like itself, and it is as broad as

with its own organs. It lives by that which nourisheth

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text: What color is it of?

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: Of it own color too.

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text: ’Tis a strange serpent.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: ’Tis so. And the tears of it are wet.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: ’Tis so. And the tears of it are wet.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: [aside to ANTONY] Will this description satisfy him?

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
[aside to CAESAR] With the health that Pompey gives
him, else he is a very epicure.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
[aside to CAESAR] With the health that Pompey gives
him, else he is a very epicure.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
[aside to MENAS] Go hang, sir, hang! Tell me of that?
Away!
Do as I bid you.—Where’s this cup I called for?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
[aside to MENAS] Go hang, sir, hang! Tell me of that?
Away!
Do as I bid you.—Where’s this cup I called for?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
[aside to MENAS] Go hang, sir, hang! Tell me of that?
Away!
Do as I bid you.—Where’s this cup I called for?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
[aside to MENAS] Go hang, sir, hang! Tell me of that?
Away!
Do as I bid you.—Where’s this cup I called for?

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
[aside to POMPEY] If for the sake of merit thou wilt
hear me,
Rise from thy stool.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: [aside to MENAS] I think th’ art mad.
He rises, and they walk aside

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: The matter?

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: I have ever held my cap off to thy fortunes.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Thou hast served me with much faith. What’s else to
say?—
[To the others] Be jolly, lords.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Thou hast served me with much faith. What’s else to
say?—
[To the others] Be jolly, lords.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
These quicksands, Lepidus,
Keep off them, for you sink.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: [aside to POMPEY] Wilt thou be lord of all the world?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: What sayst thou?

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: Wilt thou be lord of the whole world? That’s twice.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text: Wilt thou be lord of the whole world? That’s twice.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: How should that be?

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
But entertain it,
And, though thou think me poor, I am the man
Will give thee all the world.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: Hast thou drunk well?

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
No, Pompey, I have kept me from the cup.
Thou art, if thou dar’st be, the earthly Jove.
Whate’er the ocean pales or sky inclips
Is thine, if thou wilt ha ’t.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:

Thou art, if thou dar’st be, the earthly Jove.
Whate’er the ocean pales or sky inclips

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text: Show me which way.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
These three world-sharers, these competitors,
Are in thy vessel. Let me cut the cable,
And, when we are put off, fall to their throats.
All there is thine.

sentences.speaker: POMPEY
sentences.text:
Ah, this thou shouldst have done
And not have spoke on ’t! In me ’tis villainy,
In thee ’t had been good service. Thou must know,
’Tis not my profit that does lead mine honor;
Mine honor, it. Repent that e’er thy tongue
Hath so betrayed thine act. Being done unknown,
I should have found it afterwards well done,
But must condemn it now. Desist, and drink.

sentences.speaker: MENAS
sentences.text:
[aside] For this,
I’ll never follow thy palled fortunes more.
Who seeks and will not take when once ’tis offered
Shall never find it more.

Pompey.

Shall we dance now the Egyptian bacchanals
And celebrate our drink?

sentences.speaker: POMPEY

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Come, let’s all take hands
Till that the conquering wine hath steeped our sense
In soft and delicate Lethe.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
All take hands.
Make battery to our ears with the loud music,
The while I’ll place you; then the boy shall sing.
The holding every man shall beat as loud
As his strong sides can volley.

sentences.speaker: BOY
sentences.text:
(Sings) Come, thou monarch of the vine,
Plumpy Bacchus with pink eyne!
In thy vats our cares be drowned,
With thy grapes our hairs be crowned.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
What would you more? —Pompey, good night. [To ANTONY]
Good brother,
Let me request you off. Our graver business
Frowns at this levity. —Gentle lords, let’s part.
You see we have burnt our cheeks. Strong Enobarb
Is weaker than the wine; and mine own tongue
Splits what it speaks. The wild disguise hath almost
Anticked us all. What needs more words? Good night.
Good Antony, your hand.

No, to my cabin. These drums, these trumpets, flutes!

Let Neptune hear we bid a loud farewell
To these great fellows. Sound and be hanged, sound out!

sentences.speaker: VENTIDIUS
sentences.text:
Now, darting Parthia, art thou struck, and now
Pleased fortune does of Marcus Crassus’ death
Make me revenger. Bear the King’s son’s body
Before our army. Thy Pacorus, Orodes,
Pays this for Marcus Crassus.

sentences.speaker: VENTIDIUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SILIUS
sentences.text:
Noble Ventidius,
Whilst yet with Parthian blood thy sword is warm,
The fugitive Parthians follow. Spur through Media,
Mesopotamia, and the shelters whither
The routed fly. So thy grand captain, Antony,
Shall set thee on triumphant chariots and
Put garlands on thy head.

sentences.speaker: VENTIDIUS
sentences.text:
O Silius, Silius,
I have done enough. A lower place, note well,
May make too great an act. For learn this, Silius:
Better to leave undone than by our deed
Acquire too high a fame when him we serve’s away.
Caesar and Antony have ever won
More in their officer than person. Sossius,
One of my place in Syria, his lieutenant,
For quick accumulation of renown,
Which he achieved by th’ minute, lost his favor.
Who does i’ th’ wars more than his captain can
Becomes his captain’s captain; and ambition,
The soldier’s virtue, rather makes choice of loss
Than gain which darkens him.
I could do more to do Antonius good,
But ’twould offend him, and in his offense
Should my performance perish.

sentences.speaker: SILIUS
sentences.text:
Thou hast, Ventidius, that
Without the which a soldier and his sword
Grants scarce distinction. Thou wilt write to Antony?

sentences.speaker: SILIUS
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: VENTIDIUS
sentences.text:
I’ll humbly signify what in his name,
That magical word of war, we have effected:
How with his banners and his well-paid ranks
The ne’er-yet-beaten horse of Parthia
We have jaded out o’ th’ field.

sentences.speaker: SILIUS
sentences.text: Where is he now?

sentences.speaker: VENTIDIUS
sentences.text:
He purposeth to Athens, whither, with what haste
The weight we must convey with’s will permit,
We shall appear before him. [To the soliders] On,
there. Pass along!

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: What, are the brothers parted?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
They have dispatched with Pompey; he is gone.
The other three are sealing. Octavia weeps
To part from Rome. Caesar is sad, and Lepidus,
Since Pompey’s feast, as Menas says, is troubled
With the greensickness.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: A very fine one. Oh, how he loves Caesar!

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: A very fine one. Oh, how he loves Caesar!

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: Caesar? Why, he’s the Jupiter of men.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: Spake you of Caesar? How, the nonpareil!

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: Spake you of Caesar? How, the nonpareil!

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: O Antony, O thou Arabian bird!

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: Would you praise Caesar, say “Caesar.” Go no further.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Indeed, he plied them both with excellent praises.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
But he loves Caesar best; yet he loves Antony.
Hoo! Hearts, tongues, figures, scribes, bards, poets,
cannot
Think, speak, cast, write, sing, number—hoo!—
His love to Antony. But as for Caesar,
Kneel down, kneel down, and wonder.

Think, speak, cast, write, sing, number—hoo!—
His love to Antony. But as for Caesar,
Kneel down, kneel down, and wonder.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
But he loves Caesar best; yet he loves Antony.
Hoo! Hearts, tongues, figures, scribes, bards, poets,
cannot
Think, speak, cast, write, sing, number—hoo!—
His love to Antony. But as for Caesar,
Kneel down, kneel down, and wonder.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Both he loves.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: They are his shards, and he their beetle.
Trumpets within

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
So,
This is to horse. Adieu, noble Agrippa.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
So,
This is to horse. Adieu, noble Agrippa.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Good fortune, worthy soldier, and farewell.
Enter CAESAR, ANTONY, LEPIDUS, and OCTAVIA

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: No further, sir.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
You take from me a great part of myself;
Use me well in ’t. —Sister, prove such a wife
As my thoughts make thee, and as my farthest bond
Shall pass on thy approof. —Most noble Antony,
Let not the piece of virtue, which is set
Betwixt us as the cement of our love,
To keep it builded, be the ram to batter
The fortress of it. For better might we
Have loved without this mean, if on both parts
This be not cherished.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:

As my thoughts make thee, and as my farthest bond
Shall pass on thy approof. —Most noble Antony,
Let not the piece of virtue, which is set
Betwixt us as the cement of our love,
To keep it builded, be the ram to batter
The fortress of it. For better might we
Have loved without this mean, if on both parts
This be not cherished.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Make me not offended
In your distrust.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: I have said.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
You shall not find,
Though you be therein curious, the least cause
For what you seem to fear. So the gods keep you
And make the hearts of Romans serve your ends.
We will here part.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Farewell, my dearest sister, fare thee well.
The elements be kind to thee and make
Thy spirits all of comfort! Fare thee well.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: My noble brother!

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: My noble brother!

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
The April’s in her eyes; it is love’s spring,
And these the showers to bring it on. [To OCTAVIA] Be
cheerful.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
The April’s in her eyes; it is love’s spring,

cheerful.

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: [To CAESAR] Sir, look well to my husband’s house, and—

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: What, Octavia?

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: I’ll tell you in your ear.
She and CAESAR walk aside

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Her tongue will not obey her heart, nor can
Her heart inform her tongue —the swan’s-down feather
That stands upon the swell at the full of tide
And neither way inclines.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: [aside to AGRIPPA] Will Caesar weep?

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: [aside to ENOBARBUS] He has a cloud in ’s face.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
ENOBARBUS
AGRIPPA
sentences.text:
[aside to ENOBARBUS] Why, Enobarbus,
When Antony found Julius Caesar dead,
He cried almost to roaring, and he wept
When at Philippi he found Brutus slain.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
[aside to AGRIPPA] That year indeed he was troubled
with a rheum.
What willingly he did confound he wailed,
Believe ’t, till I wept too.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:

with a rheum.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
[coming forward with OCTAVIA] No, sweet Octavia,
You shall hear from me still. The time shall not
Outgo my thinking on you.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Come, sir, come,
I’ll wrestle with you in my strength of love.
Look, here I have you.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR

sentences.speaker: CAESAR

sentences.speaker: LEPIDUS
sentences.text:
[To ANTONY] Let all the number of the stars give light
To thy fair way.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: Farewell, farewell.
Kisses OCTAVIA

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: Farewell.
Trumpets sound. Exeunt

Enter CLEOPATRA, CHARMIAN, IRAS, and ALEXAS

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Where is the fellow?

sentences.speaker: ALEXAS
sentences.text: Half afeard to come.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Go to, go to.—Come hither, sir.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Go to, go to.—Come hither, sir.
Enter the MESSENGER as before

sentences.speaker: ALEXAS
sentences.text:
Good majesty,
Herod of Jewry dare not look upon you
But when you are well pleased.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
That Herod’s head
I’ll have! But how? When Antony is gone,
Through whom I might command it? [To MESSENGER] Come
thou near.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Most gracious majesty!

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Didst thou behold Octavia?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Ay, dread Queen.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Where?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Madam, in Rome.
I looked her in the face, and saw her led
Between her brother and Mark Antony.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Madam, in Rome.
I looked her in the face, and saw her led
Between her brother and Mark Antony.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Is she as tall as me?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: She is not, madam.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Didst hear her speak? Is she shrill-tongued or low?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Didst hear her speak? Is she shrill-tongued or low?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Madam, I heard her speak. She is low-voiced.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Madam, I heard her speak. She is low-voiced.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: That’s not so good. He cannot like her long.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: That’s not so good. He cannot like her long.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text: Like her? O Isis, ’tis impossible.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text: Like her? O Isis, ’tis impossible.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
I think so, Charmian. Dull of tongue, and dwarfish.—
What majesty is in her gait? Remember,
If e’er thou looked’st on majesty.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
She creeps.
Her motion and her station are as one.
She shows a body rather than a life,
A statue than a breather.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Or I have no observance.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text:
Three in Egypt
Cannot make better note.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
He’s very knowing,
I do perceive ’t. There’s nothing in her yet.
The fellow has good judgment.

I do perceive ’t. There’s nothing in her yet.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text: Excellent.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: [To MESSENGER] Guess at her years, I prithee.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Madam, she was a widow—

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Widow? Charmian, hark.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Widow? Charmian, hark.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: And I do think she’s thirty.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Bear’st thou her face in mind? Is ’t long or round?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: Bear’st thou her face in mind? Is ’t long or round?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: Round, even to faultiness.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
For the most part, too, they are foolish that are so.
Her hair, what color?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
For the most part, too, they are foolish that are so.
Her hair, what color?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Brown, madam, and her forehead
As low as she would wish it.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
[giving money] There’s gold for thee.
Thou must not take my former sharpness ill.
I will employ thee back again; I find thee
Most fit for business. Go make thee ready;
Our letters are prepared.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text: A proper man.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Indeed, he is so. I repent me much
That so I harried him. Why, methinks, by him,
This creature’s no such thing.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text: Nothing, madam.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: The man hath seen some majesty and should know.

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text:
Hath he seen majesty? Isis else defend,
And serving you so long!

sentences.speaker: CHARMIAN
sentences.text:
Hath he seen majesty? Isis else defend,

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
I have one thing more to ask him yet, good Charmian—
But ’tis no matter; thou shalt bring him to me
Where I will write. All may be well enough.

Enter ANTONY and OCTAVIA

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Nay, nay, Octavia, not only that—
That were excusable, that and thousands more
Of semblable import —but he hath waged
New wars ’gainst Pompey; made his will, and read it
To public ear;
Spoke scantly of me; when perforce he could not
But pay me terms of honor, cold and sickly
He vented them, most narrow measure lent me.
When the best hint was given him, he not took ’t,
Or did it from his teeth.

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text:
O my good lord,
Believe not all, or, if you must believe,
Stomach not all. A more unhappy lady,
If this division chance, ne’er stood between,
Praying for both parts.
The good gods will mock me presently,
When I shall pray “O bless my lord and husband!”
Undo that prayer by crying out as loud
“O bless my brother!” Husband win, win brother
Prays and destroys the prayer; no midway
’Twixt these extremes at all.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Gentle Octavia,
Let your best love draw to that point which seeks
Best to preserve it. If I lose mine honor,
I lose myself; better I were not yours
Than yours so branchless. But, as you requested,
Yourself shall go between ’s. The meantime, lady,
I’ll raise the preparation of a war
Shall stain your brother. Make your soonest haste;
So your desires are yours.

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text:
Thanks to my lord.
The Jove of power make me most weak, most weak,
Your reconciler! Wars ’twixt you twain would be
As if the world should cleave, and that slain men
Should solder up the rift.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
When it appears to you where this begins,
Turn your displeasure that way, for our faults
Can never be so equal that your love
Can equally move with them. Provide your going;
Choose your own company and command what cost
Your heart has mind to.

Enter ENOBARBUS and EROS

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: How now, friend Eros!

sentences.speaker: EROS
sentences.text: There’s strange news come, sir.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: What, man?

sentences.speaker: EROS
sentences.text: Caesar and Lepidus have made wars upon Pompey.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: This is old. What is the success?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: This is old. What is the success?

sentences.speaker: EROS
sentences.text:
Caesar, having made use of him in the wars ’gainst
Pompey, presently denied him rivality, would not let him
partake in the glory of the action, and, not resting
here, accuses him of letters he had formerly wrote to
Pompey; upon his own appeal seizes him. So the poor
third is up, till death enlarge his confine.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Then, world, thou hast a pair of chaps, no more,
And throw between them all the food thou hast,
They’ll grind the one the other. Where’s Antony?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Then, world, thou hast a pair of chaps, no more,
And throw between them all the food thou hast,
They’ll grind the one the other. Where’s Antony?

sentences.speaker: EROS
sentences.text:
He’s walking in the garden— (imitating anger) thus, and
spurns
The rush that lies before him; cries, “Fool Lepidus!”
And threats the throat of that his officer
That murdered Pompey.

The rush that lies before him; cries, “Fool Lepidus!”

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: Our great navy’s rigged.

sentences.speaker: EROS
sentences.text:
For Italy and Caesar. More, Domitius:
My lord desires you presently. My news
I might have told hereafter.

sentences.speaker: EROS
sentences.text:
For Italy and Caesar. More, Domitius:
My lord desires you presently. My news
I might have told hereafter.

sentences.speaker: EROS
sentences.text:
For Italy and Caesar. More, Domitius:
My lord desires you presently. My news
I might have told hereafter.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
’Twill be naught,
But let it be. Bring me to Antony.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
’Twill be naught,
But let it be. Bring me to Antony.

sentences.speaker: EROS
sentences.text: Come, sir.
Exeunt
Enter AGRIPPA, MECAENAS, and CAESAR

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Contemning Rome, he has done all this and more
In Alexandria. Here’s the manner of ’t:
I’ th’ marketplace, on a tribunal silvered,
Cleopatra and himself in chairs of gold
Were publicly enthroned. At the feet sat
Caesarion, whom they call my father’s son,
And all the unlawful issue that their lust
Since then hath made between them. Unto her
He gave the stablishment of Egypt, made her
Of lower Syria, Cyprus, Lydia,
Absolute Queen.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text: This in the public eye?

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
I’ th’ common showplace, where they exercise.
His sons he there proclaimed the kings of kings:
Great Media, Parthia, and Armenia
He gave to Alexander; to Ptolemy he assigned
Syria, Cilicia, and Phoenicia . She
In th’ habiliments of the goddess Isis
That day appeared, and oft before gave audience,
As ’tis reported, so.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text: Let Rome be thus informed.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text:
Who, queasy with his insolence already,
Will their good thoughts call from him.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
The people knows it, and have now received
His accusations.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Who does he accuse?

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Caesar, and that, having in Sicily
Sextus Pompeius spoiled, we had not rated him
His part o’ th’ isle. Then does he say he lent me
Some shipping, unrestored. Lastly, he frets
That Lepidus of the triumvirate
Should be deposed, and, being, that we detain
All his revenue.

Should be deposed, and, being, that we detain

Caesar, and that, having in Sicily

His part o’ th’ isle. Then does he say he lent me
Some shipping, unrestored. Lastly, he frets
That Lepidus of the triumvirate
Should be deposed, and, being, that we detain
All his revenue.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Sir, this should be answered.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
’Tis done already, and the messenger gone.
I have told him, Lepidus was grown too cruel,
That he his high authority abused
And did deserve his change. For what I have conquered,
I grant him part; but then, in his Armenia,
And other of his conquered kingdoms, I
Demand the like.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text: He’ll never yield to that.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: Nor must not then be yielded to in this.
Enter OCTAVIA with her train

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: Hail, Caesar, and my lord! Hail, most dear Caesar!

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: Hail, Caesar, and my lord! Hail, most dear Caesar!

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: That ever I should call thee castaway!

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: You have not called me so, nor have you cause.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Why have you stol’n upon us thus? You come not
Like Caesar’s sister. The wife of Antony
Should have an army for an usher and
The neighs of horse to tell of her approach
Long ere she did appear. The trees by th’ way
Should have borne men, and expectation fainted,
Longing for what it had not. Nay, the dust
Should have ascended to the roof of heaven,
Raised by your populous troops. But you are come
A market-maid to Rome and have prevented
The ostentation of our love, which, left unshown,
Is often left unloved. We should have met you
By sea and land, supplying every stage
With an augmented greeting.

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text:
Good my lord,
To come thus was I not constrained, but did it
On my free will. My lord, Mark Antony,
Hearing that you prepared for war, acquainted
My grievèd ear withal, whereon I begged
His pardon for return.

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text:
Good my lord,
To come thus was I not constrained, but did it
On my free will. My lord, Mark Antony,
Hearing that you prepared for war, acquainted
My grievèd ear withal, whereon I begged
His pardon for return.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Which soon he granted,
Being an obstruct ’tween his lust and him.

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: Do not say so, my lord.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
I have eyes upon him,
And his affairs come to me on the wind.
Where is he now?

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
I have eyes upon him,
And his affairs come to me on the wind.
Where is he now?

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: My lord, in Athens.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
No, my most wrongèd sister. Cleopatra
Hath nodded him to her. He hath given his empire
Up to a whore; who now are levying
The kings o’ th’ earth for war. He hath assembled
Bocchus, the King of Libya; Archelaus,
Of Cappadocia; Philadelphos, King
Of Paphlagonia; the Thracian king, Adallas;
King Manchus of Arabia; King of Pont;
Herod of Jewry; Mithridates, King
Of Comagen; Polemon and Amyntas,
The Kings of Mede and Lycaonia,
With a more larger list of scepters.

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text:
Ay me, most wretched,
That have my heart parted betwixt two friends
That does afflict each other!

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Welcome hither.
Your letters did withhold our breaking forth
Till we perceived both how you were wrong led
And we in negligent danger. Cheer your heart.
Be you not troubled with the time, which drives
O’er your content these strong necessities,
But let determined things to destiny
Hold unbewailed their way. Welcome to Rome,
Nothing more dear to me. You are abused
Beyond the mark of thought, and the high gods,
To do you justice, makes his ministers
Of us and those that love you. Best of comfort,
And ever welcome to us.

sentences.speaker: AGRIPPA
sentences.text: Welcome, lady.

sentences.speaker: MAECENAS
sentences.text:
Welcome, dear madam.
Each heart in Rome does love and pity you.
Only th’ adulterous Antony, most large
In his abominations, turns you off
And gives his potent regiment to a trull
That noises it against us.

sentences.speaker: OCTAVIA
sentences.text: [To CAESAR] Is it so, sir?

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Most certain. Sister, welcome. Pray you
Be ever known to patience. My dear’st sister!

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Most certain. Sister, welcome. Pray you
Be ever known to patience. My dear’st sister!

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:

Exeunt
Enter CLEOPATRA and ENOBARBUS

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: I will be even with thee, doubt it not.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: But why, why, why?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Thou hast forspoke my being in these wars
And sayst it is not fit.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: Well, is it, is it?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Is ’t not denounced against us? Why should not we
Be there in person?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Is ’t not denounced against us? Why should not we
Be there in person?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Well, I could reply,
If we should serve with horse and mares together,
The horse were merely lost. The mares would bear
A soldier and his horse.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Well, I could reply,
If we should serve with horse and mares together,
The horse were merely lost. The mares would bear
A soldier and his horse.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: What is ’t you say?

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Your presence needs must puzzle Antony,
Take from his heart, take from his brain, from ’s time
What should not then be spared. He is already
Traduced for levity, and ’tis said in Rome
That Photinus, an eunuch, and your maids
Manage this war.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:

That Photinus, an eunuch, and your maids
Manage this war.

That Photinus, an eunuch, and your maids
Manage this war.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Sink Rome! And their tongues rot
That speak against us! A charge we bear i’ th’ war,
And as the president of my kingdom will
Appear there for a man. Speak not against it.
I will not stay behind.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Sink Rome! And their tongues rot
That speak against us! A charge we bear i’ th’ war,
And as the president of my kingdom will
Appear there for a man. Speak not against it.
I will not stay behind.
Enter ANTONY and CANIDIUS

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Nay, I have done.
Here comes the Emperor.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Nay, I have done.
Here comes the Emperor.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Is it not strange, Canidius,
That from Tarentum and Brundusium
He could so quickly cut the Ionian sea
And take in Toryne? —You have heard on ’t, sweet?

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text:
Celerity is never more admired
Than by the negligent.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
A good rebuke,
Which might have well becomed the best of men,
To taunt at slackness. —Canidius, we will fight
With him by sea.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: By sea, what else?

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text:
Why will
My lord do so?

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: For that he dares us to ’t.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: So hath my lord dared him to single fight.

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text:
Ay, and to wage this battle at Pharsalia,
Where Caesar fought with Pompey. But these offers,
Which serve not for his vantage, he shakes off,
And so should you.

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text:
Ay, and to wage this battle at Pharsalia,
Where Caesar fought with Pompey. But these offers,
Which serve not for his vantage, he shakes off,
And so should you.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Your ships are not well manned,
Your mariners are muleteers, reapers, people
Engrossed by swift impress. In Caesar’s fleet
Are those that often have ’gainst Pompey fought.
Their ships are yare, yours, heavy. No disgrace
Shall fall you for refusing him at sea,
Being prepared for land.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:

Engrossed by swift impress. In Caesar’s fleet
Are those that often have ’gainst Pompey fought.
Their ships are yare, yours, heavy. No disgrace
Shall fall you for refusing him at sea,
Being prepared for land.

sentences.text:

Your mariners are muleteers, reapers, people
Engrossed by swift impress. In Caesar’s fleet
Are those that often have ’gainst Pompey fought.
Their ships are yare, yours, heavy. No disgrace
Shall fall you for refusing him at sea,
Being prepared for land.

sentences.text:

Are those that often have ’gainst Pompey fought.
Their ships are yare, yours, heavy. No disgrace

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Your ships are not well manned,
Your mariners are muleteers, reapers, people
Engrossed by swift impress. In Caesar’s fleet
Are those that often have ’gainst Pompey fought.
Their ships are yare, yours, heavy. No disgrace

Being prepared for land.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: By sea, by sea.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Most worthy sir, you therein throw away
The absolute soldiership you have by land,
Distract your army, which doth most consist
Of war-marked footmen, leave unexecuted
Your own renownèd knowledge, quite forego
The way which promises assurance, and
Give up yourself merely to chance and hazard
From firm security.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: I’ll fight at sea.

sentences.speaker: CLEOPATRA
sentences.text: I have sixty sails, Caesar none better.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Our overplus of shipping will we burn,
And with the rest full-manned, from th’ head of Actium
Beat th’ approaching Caesar. But if we fail,
We then can do ’t at land.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Our overplus of shipping will we burn,
And with the rest full-manned, from th’ head of Actium
Beat th’ approaching Caesar. But if we fail,
We then can do ’t at land.
Enter a MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: Thy business?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
The news is true, my lord. He is descried.
Caesar has taken Toryne.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
The news is true, my lord. He is descried.
Caesar has taken Toryne.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
The news is true, my lord. He is descried.
Caesar has taken Toryne.
Exit

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Can he be there in person? ’Tis impossible,
Strange that his power should be. Canidius,
Our nineteen legions thou shalt hold by land,
And our twelve thousand horse. We’ll to our ship.
Away, my Thetis!

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Can he be there in person? ’Tis impossible,
Strange that his power should be. Canidius,
Our nineteen legions thou shalt hold by land,
And our twelve thousand horse. We’ll to our ship.
Away, my Thetis!

sentences.text:

Strange that his power should be. Canidius,
Our nineteen legions thou shalt hold by land,
And our twelve thousand horse. We’ll to our ship.
Away, my Thetis!

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Can he be there in person? ’Tis impossible,
Strange that his power should be. Canidius,
Our nineteen legions thou shalt hold by land,
And our twelve thousand horse. We’ll to our ship.
Away, my Thetis!
Enter a SOLDIER

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: How now, worthy soldier?

sentences.speaker: SOLDIER
sentences.text:
O noble Emperor, do not fight by sea!
Trust not to rotten planks. Do you misdoubt
This sword and these my wounds? Let th’ Egyptians
And the Phoenicians go a-ducking. We
Have used to conquer standing on the earth
And fighting foot to foot.

sentences.speaker: SOLDIER
sentences.text:
O noble Emperor, do not fight by sea!
Trust not to rotten planks. Do you misdoubt
This sword and these my wounds? Let th’ Egyptians
And the Phoenicians go a-ducking. We
Have used to conquer standing on the earth
And fighting foot to foot.

Trust not to rotten planks. Do you misdoubt
This sword and these my wounds? Let th’ Egyptians
And the Phoenicians go a-ducking. We
Have used to conquer standing on the earth
And fighting foot to foot.

And the Phoenicians go a-ducking. We
Have used to conquer standing on the earth

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text: Well, well, away.
Exeunt ANTONY, CLEOPATRA, and ENOBARBUS

sentences.speaker: SOLDIER
sentences.text: By Hercules, I think I am i’ th’ right.

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text:
Soldier, thou art; but his whole action grows
Not in the power on ’t. So our leader’s led,
And we are women’s men.

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text:
Soldier, thou art; but his whole action grows
Not in the power on ’t. So our leader’s led,
And we are women’s men.

sentences.speaker: SOLDIER
sentences.text:
You keep by land
The legions and the horse whole, do you not?

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text:
Marcus Octavius, Marcus Justeius,
Publicola, and Caelius, are for sea;
But we keep whole by land. This speed of Caesar’s
Carries beyond belief.

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text:
Marcus Octavius, Marcus Justeius,
Publicola, and Caelius, are for sea;
But we keep whole by land. This speed of Caesar’s
Carries beyond belief.

sentences.speaker: SOLDIER
sentences.text:
While he was yet in Rome
His power went out in such distractions as
Beguiled all spies.

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text: Who’s his lieutenant, hear you?

sentences.speaker: SOLDIER
sentences.text: They say, one Taurus.

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text: Well I know the man.
Enter a MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text: The Emperor calls Canidius.

sentences.speaker: CANIDIUS
sentences.text:
With news the time’s with labor, and throws forth
Each minute some.
Exeunt
Enter CAESAR with his army, and TAURUS, marching

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text: Taurus!

sentences.speaker: TAURUS
sentences.text: My lord?

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Strike not by land; keep whole. Provoke not battle
Till we have done at sea. Do not exceed
The prescript of this scroll.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Strike not by land; keep whole. Provoke not battle
Till we have done at sea. Do not exceed
The prescript of this scroll.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:
Strike not by land; keep whole. Provoke not battle
Till we have done at sea. Do not exceed
The prescript of this scroll.

sentences.text:
Strike not by land; keep whole. Provoke not battle
Till we have done at sea. Do not exceed
The prescript of this scroll.

sentences.text:
Strike not by land; keep whole. Provoke not battle
Till we have done at sea. Do not exceed
The prescript of this scroll.

sentences.speaker: CAESAR
sentences.text:

Till we have done at sea. Do not exceed
The prescript of this scroll.
Exit with army
Enter ANTONY and ENOBARBUS

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Set we our squadrons on yond side o’ th’ hill,
In eye of Caesar’s battle, from which place
We may the number of the ships behold
And so proceed accordingly.

sentences.speaker: ANTONY
sentences.text:
Set we our squadrons on yond side o’ th’ hill,
In eye of Caesar’s battle, from which place
We may the number of the ships behold
And so proceed accordingly.
Exit
CANIDIUS marcheth with his land army one way over the stage, and TAURUS, the lieutenant of CAESAR, the other way. After their going in is heard the noise of a sea fight

Alarum. Enter ENOBARBUS
Alarum. Enter ENOBARBUS

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
Naught, naught, all naught! I can behold no longer.
Th’ Antoniad , the Egyptian admiral,
With all their sixty, fly and turn the rudder.
To see ’t mine eyes are blasted.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:

With all their sixty, fly and turn the rudder.

Enter SCARUS

sentences.speaker: SCARUS
sentences.text:
Gods and goddesses,
All the whole synod of them!

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: What’s thy passion?

sentences.speaker: SCARUS
sentences.text:
The greater cantle of the world is lost
With very ignorance. We have kissed away
Kingdoms and provinces.

sentences.speaker: SCARUS
sentences.text:
The greater cantle of the world is lost
With very ignorance. We have kissed away
Kingdoms and provinces.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text: How appears the fight?

sentences.speaker: SCARUS
sentences.text:
On our side like the tokened pestilence,
Where death is sure. Yon ribaudred nag of Egypt—
Whom leprosy o’ertake!—i’ th’ midst o’ th’ fight,
When vantage like a pair of twins appeared
Both as the same, or rather ours the elder,
The breeze upon her, like a cow in June,
Hoists sails and flies.

sentences.speaker: SCARUS
sentences.text:
On our side like the tokened pestilence,
Where death is sure. Yon ribaudred nag of Egypt—
Whom leprosy o’ertake!—i’ th’ midst o’ th’ fight,
When vantage like a pair of twins appeared
Both as the same, or rather ours the elder,
The breeze upon her, like a cow in June,
Hoists sails and flies.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
That I beheld.
Mine eyes did sicken at the sight and could not
Endure a further view.

sentences.speaker: ENOBARBUS
sentences.text:
That I beheld.
Mine eyes did sicken at the sight and could not
Endure a further view.

sentences.speaker: SCARUS
sentences.text:
She once being loofed,
The noble ruin of her magic, Antony,
Claps on his sea-wing and, like a doting mallard
Leaving the fight in height, flies after her.
I never saw an action of such shame.
Experience, manhood, honor, ne’er before
Did violate so itself.

sentences.speaker: SCARUS
sentences.text:
She once being loofed,
The noble ruin of her magic, Antony,
Claps on his sea-wing and, like a doting mallard
Leaving the fight in height, flies after her.
I never saw an action of such shame.
Experience, manhood, honor, ne’er before
Did violate so itself.
